           Branch data     Line data    Source code
       1                 :            : /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
       2                 :            :  *
       3                 :            :  * postmaster.c
       4                 :            :  *        This program acts as a clearing house for requests to the
       5                 :            :  *        POSTGRES system.      Frontend programs send a startup message
       6                 :            :  *        to the Postmaster and the postmaster uses the info in the
       7                 :            :  *        message to setup a backend process.
       8                 :            :  *
       9                 :            :  *        The postmaster also manages system-wide operations such as
      10                 :            :  *        startup and shutdown. The postmaster itself doesn't do those
      11                 :            :  *        operations, mind you --- it just forks off a subprocess to do them
      12                 :            :  *        at the right times.  It also takes care of resetting the system
      13                 :            :  *        if a backend crashes.
      14                 :            :  *
      15                 :            :  *        The postmaster process creates the shared memory and semaphore
      16                 :            :  *        pools during startup, but as a rule does not touch them itself.
      17                 :            :  *        In particular, it is not a member of the PGPROC array of backends
      18                 :            :  *        and so it cannot participate in lock-manager operations.      Keeping
      19                 :            :  *        the postmaster away from shared memory operations makes it simpler
      20                 :            :  *        and more reliable.  The postmaster is almost always able to recover
      21                 :            :  *        from crashes of individual backends by resetting shared memory;
      22                 :            :  *        if it did much with shared memory then it would be prone to crashing
      23                 :            :  *        along with the backends.
      24                 :            :  *
      25                 :            :  *        When a request message is received, we now fork() immediately.
      26                 :            :  *        The child process performs authentication of the request, and
      27                 :            :  *        then becomes a backend if successful.  This allows the auth code
      28                 :            :  *        to be written in a simple single-threaded style (as opposed to the
      29                 :            :  *        crufty "poor man's multitasking" code that used to be needed).
      30                 :            :  *        More importantly, it ensures that blockages in non-multithreaded
      31                 :            :  *        libraries like SSL or PAM cannot cause denial of service to other
      32                 :            :  *        clients.
      33                 :            :  *
      34                 :            :  *
      35                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 2005-2009, Greenplum inc
      36                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2009, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
      37                 :            :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
      38                 :            :  *
      39                 :            :  *
      40                 :            :  * IDENTIFICATION
      41                 :            :  *        $PostgreSQL: pgsql/src/backend/postmaster/postmaster.c,v 1.612 2010/06/16 00:54:16 petere Exp $
      42                 :            :  *
      43                 :            :  * NOTES
      44                 :            :  *
      45                 :            :  * Initialization:
      46                 :            :  *              The Postmaster sets up shared memory data structures
      47                 :            :  *              for the backends.
      48                 :            :  *
      49                 :            :  * Synchronization:
      50                 :            :  *              The Postmaster shares memory with the backends but should avoid
      51                 :            :  *              touching shared memory, so as not to become stuck if a crashing
      52                 :            :  *              backend screws up locks or shared memory.  Likewise, the Postmaster
      53                 :            :  *              should never block on messages from frontend clients.
      54                 :            :  *
      55                 :            :  * Garbage Collection:
      56                 :            :  *              The Postmaster cleans up after backends if they have an emergency
      57                 :            :  *              exit and/or core dump.
      58                 :            :  *
      59                 :            :  * Error Reporting:
      60                 :            :  *              Use write_stderr() only for reporting "interactive" errors
      61                 :            :  *              (essentially, bogus arguments on the command line).  Once the
      62                 :            :  *              postmaster is launched, use ereport().  In particular, don't use
      63                 :            :  *              write_stderr() for anything that occurs after pmdaemonize.
      64                 :            :  *
      65                 :            :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
      66                 :            :  */
      67                 :            :

     159                 :            : /*
     160                 :            :  * List of active backends (or child processes anyway; we don't actually
     161                 :            :  * know whether a given child has become a backend or is still in the
     162                 :            :  * authorization phase).  This is used mainly to keep track of how many
     163                 :            :  * children we have and send them appropriate signals when necessary.
     164                 :            :  *
     165                 :            :  * "Special" children such as the startup, bgwriter and autovacuum launcher
     166                 :            :  * tasks are not in this list.  Autovacuum worker processes are in it.
     167                 :            :  * Also, "dead_end" children are in it: these are children launched just
     168                 :            :  * for the purpose of sending a friendly rejection message to a would-be
     169                 :            :  * client.      We must track them because they are attached to shared memory,
     170                 :            :  * but we know they will never become live backends.  dead_end children are
     171                 :            :  * not assigned a PMChildSlot.
     172                 :            :  */
     173                 :            : typedef struct bkend
     174                 :            : {
     175                 :            :         pid_t           pid;                    /* process id of backend */
     176                 :            :         long            cancel_key;             /* cancel key for cancels for this backend */
     177                 :            :         int                     child_slot;             /* PMChildSlot for this backend, if any */
     178                 :            :         bool            is_autovacuum;  /* is it an autovacuum process? */
     179                 :            :         bool            dead_end;               /* is it going to send an error and quit? */
     180                 :            :         Dlelem          elem;                   /* list link in BackendList */
     181                 :            : } Backend;
     182                 :            : 
     183                 :            : static Dllist *BackendList;

     190                 :            :         FtsProbeProc,
     191                 :            :         PerfmonProc,
     192                 :            :         BackoffProc,
     193                 :            :         PerfmonSegmentInfoProc,
     194                 :            :         MaxPMSubType
     195                 :            : } PMSubType;

     198                 :            : static Backend *ShmemBackendArray;
     199                 :            : #endif

     202                 :            : int                     PostPortNumber;

     205                 :            : 
     206                 :            : /*
     207                 :            :  * ReservedBackends is the number of backends reserved for superuser use.
     208                 :            :  * This number is taken out of the pool size given by MaxBackends so
     209                 :            :  * number of backend slots available to non-superusers is
     210                 :            :  * (MaxBackends - ReservedBackends).  Note what this really means is
     211                 :            :  * "if there are <= ReservedBackends connections available, only superusers
     212                 :            :  * can make new connections" --- pre-existing superuser connections don't
     213                 :            :  * count against the limit.
     214                 :            :  */
     215                 :            : int                     ReservedBackends;
     216                 :            :

     226                 :            : /*
     227                 :            :  * These globals control the behavior of the postmaster in case some
     228                 :            :  * backend dumps core.  Normally, it kills all peers of the dead backend
     229                 :            :  * and reinitializes shared memory.  By specifying -s or -n, we can have
     230                 :            :  * the postmaster stop (rather than kill) peers and not reinitialize
     231                 :            :  * shared data structures.      (Reinit is currently dead code, though.)
     232                 :            :  */

     234                 :            : static int      SendStop = false;

     276                 :            : 
     277                 :            : /* Startup/shutdown state */
     278                 :            : #define                 NoShutdown              0
     279                 :            : #define                 SmartShutdown   1
     280                 :            : #define                 FastShutdown    2
     281                 :            : #define                 ImmediateShutdown       3
     282                 :            : 
     283                 :            : static int      Shutdown = NoShutdown;
     284                 :            : 
     285                 :            : /* T if recovering from a backend crash, up to the point of resetting shared memory and starting up again
     286                 :            :  * (or going into quiescent mode if it's a primary/mirror that has reset) */
     287                 :            : static bool FatalError = false;
     288                 :            : static bool RecoveryError = false;              /* T if WAL recovery failed */
     289                 :            : static int pmResetCounter = 0;
     290                 :            : 
     291                 :            : static bool gHaveCalledOnetimeInitFunctions = false;
     292                 :            : 
     293                 :            : /*
     294                 :            :  * We use a simple state machine to control startup, shutdown, and
     295                 :            :  * crash recovery (which is rather like shutdown followed by startup).
     296                 :            :  *
     297                 :            :  * After doing all the postmaster initialization work, we enter PM_STARTUP
     298                 :            :  * state and the startup process is launched. The startup process begins by
     299                 :            :  * reading the control file and other preliminary initialization steps.
     300                 :            :  * In a normal startup, or after crash recovery, the startup process exits
     301                 :            :  * with exit code 0 and we switch to PM_RUN state.  However, archive recovery
     302                 :            :  * is handled specially since it takes much longer and we would like to support
     303                 :            :  * hot standby during archive recovery.
     304                 :            :  *
     305                 :            :  * When the startup process is ready to start archive recovery, it signals the
     306                 :            :  * postmaster, and we switch to PM_RECOVERY state. The background writer is
     307                 :            :  * launched, while the startup process continues applying WAL.
     308                 :            :  * After reaching a consistent point in WAL redo, startup process signals
     309                 :            :  * us again, and we switch to PM_RECOVERY_CONSISTENT state. There's currently
     310                 :            :  * no difference between PM_RECOVERY and PM_RECOVERY_CONSISTENT, but we
     311                 :            :  * could start accepting connections to perform read-only queries at this
     312                 :            :  * point, if we had the infrastructure to do that.
     313                 :            :  * When archive recovery is finished, the startup process exits with exit
     314                 :            :  * code 0 and we switch to PM_RUN state.
     315                 :            :  *
     316                 :            :  * Normal child backends can only be launched when we are in PM_RUN state.
     317                 :            :  * (We also allow it in PM_WAIT_BACKUP state, but only for superusers.)
     318                 :            :  * In other states we handle connection requests by launching "dead_end"
     319                 :            :  * child processes, which will simply send the client an error message and
     320                 :            :  * quit.  (We track these in the BackendList so that we can know when they
     321                 :            :  * are all gone; this is important because they're still connected to shared
     322                 :            :  * memory, and would interfere with an attempt to destroy the shmem segment,
     323                 :            :  * possibly leading to SHMALL failure when we try to make a new one.)
     324                 :            :  * In PM_WAIT_DEAD_END state we are waiting for all the dead_end children
     325                 :            :  * to drain out of the system, and therefore stop accepting connection
     326                 :            :  * requests at all until the last existing child has quit (which hopefully
     327                 :            :  * will not be very long).
     328                 :            :  *
     329                 :            :  * Notice that this state variable does not distinguish *why* we entered
     330                 :            :  * states later than PM_RUN --- Shutdown and FatalError must be consulted
     331                 :            :  * to find that out.  FatalError is never true in PM_INIT through PM_RUN
     332                 :            :  * states, nor in PM_SHUTDOWN states (because we don't enter those states
     333                 :            :  * when trying to recover from a crash).
     334                 :            :  *
     335                 :            :  * RecoveryError means that we have crashed during recovery, and
     336                 :            :  * should not try to restart.
     337                 :            :  */
     338                 :            : typedef enum
     339                 :            : {
     340                 :            :         PM_INIT,                                        /* postmaster starting */
     341                 :            :         PM_STARTUP,                                     /* waiting for startup subprocess */
     342                 :            :         PM_STARTUP_PASS2,                       /* waiting for startup pass 2 subprocess */
     343                 :            :         PM_STARTUP_PASS3,                       /* waiting for startup pass 3 subprocess */
     344                 :            :         PM_STARTUP_PASS4,                       /* waiting for startup pass 4 subprocess */
     345                 :            :         PM_RECOVERY,                            /* in archive recovery mode */
     346                 :            :         PM_RECOVERY_CONSISTENT,         /* consistent recovery mode */
     347                 :            :         PM_RUN,                                         /* normal "database is alive" state */
     348                 :            :         //PM_WAIT_BACKUP,                               /* waiting for online backup mode to end */
     349                 :            : 
     350                 :            :     /** ORDER OF SHUTDOWN ENUMS MATTERS: we move through the states by adding 1 to the current state */
     351                 :            :     /* shutdown has begun. */
     352                 :            :     PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN,
     353                 :            : 
     354                 :            :     /* check to make sure startup processes are done */
     355                 :            :         PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_STARTUP_PROCESSES,
     356                 :            : 
     357                 :            :     /* waiting for live backends to exit */
     358                 :            :         PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BACKENDS,
     359                 :            : 
     360                 :            :         /* waiting for pre-bgwriter services to exit */
     361                 :            :         PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_PREBGWRITER,
     362                 :            : 
     363                 :            :         /* waiting for bgwriter to do shutdown ckpt */
     364                 :            :         PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BGWRITER_CHECKPOINT,
     365                 :            : 
     366                 :            :     /* waiting for post-bgwriter services */
     367                 :            :     PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_POSTBGWRITER,
     368                 :            : 
     369                 :            :         /* waiting for dead_end children to exit */
     370                 :            :         PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_DEAD_END_CHILDREN,
     371                 :            : 
     372                 :            :         /* all important children have exited */
     373                 :            :         PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_NO_CHILDREN,
     374                 :            : 
     375                 :            :         /* this is NOT a stopping state; this state is used after everything has been stopped for postmaster reset
     376                 :            :          *   during mirroring -- if we should reset the peer as well and start the system back up, we enter this
     377                 :            :          *   state */
     378                 :            :         PM_POSTMASTER_RESET_FILEREP_PEER,
     379                 :            : 
     380                 :            :     PM__ENUMERATION_COUNT
     381                 :            : } PMState;
     382                 :            : 
     383                 :            : /**
     384                 :            :  * WARNING: some of these labels are used in management scripts -- so don't
     385                 :            :  *  change them here without checking the management scripts (check for starting with child_stop_
     386                 :            :  *  as well -- management scripts use startWith in some cases)
     387                 :            :  */
     388                 :            : static const char *const gPmStateLabels[] =
     389                 :            : {
     390                 :            :         "init",
     391                 :            :     "startup",
     392                 :            :     "startup_pass2",
     393                 :            :     "startup_pass3",
     394                 :            :         "startup_pass4",
     395                 :            :     "recovery",
     396                 :            :     "recovery_consistent",
     397                 :            :     "run",
     398                 :            : 
     399                 :            :     "child_stop_begin",
     400                 :            :     "child_stop_wait_startup_processes",
     401                 :            :     "child_stop_wait_backends",
     402                 :            :     "child_stop_wait_pre_bgwriter",
     403                 :            :     "child_stop_bgwriter_checkpoint",
     404                 :            :     "child_stop_post_bgwriter",
     405                 :            :     "child_stop_dead_end_children",
     406                 :            :     "child_stop_no_children",
     407                 :            : 
     408                 :            :     "reset_filerep_peer"
     409                 :            : };
     410                 :            : 
     411                 :            : typedef enum
     412                 :            : {
     413                 :            :         PEER_RESET_NONE,
     414                 :            :         PEER_RESET_FAILED,
     415                 :            :         PEER_RESET_SUCCEEDED,
     416                 :            : } PeerResetResult;
     417                 :            : 
     418                 :            : static PeerResetResult peerResetResult = PEER_RESET_NONE;
     419                 :            : 
     420                 :            : static PMState pmState = PM_INIT;
     421                 :            : 
     422                 :            : /**
     423                 :            :  * We set this to true to force the state machine to run the actions for transitioning to its current state,
     424                 :            :  *  even if the state is not changed
     425                 :            :  */
     426                 :            : static bool pmStateMachineMustRunActions = false;
     427                 :            : 
     428                 :            : 
     429                 :            : /* CDB */
     430                 :            : 
     431                 :            : typedef enum PMSUBPROC_FLAGS
     432                 :            : {
     433                 :            :         PMSUBPROC_FLAG_QD                                       = 0x1,
     434                 :            :         PMSUBPROC_FLAG_STANDBY                          = 0x2,
     435                 :            :         PMSUBPROC_FLAG_QE                                       = 0x4,
     436                 :            :         PMSUBPROC_FLAG_QD_AND_QE                        = (PMSUBPROC_FLAG_QD|PMSUBPROC_FLAG_QE),
     437                 :            :         PMSUBPROC_FLAG_STOP_AFTER_BGWRITER      = 0x8,
     438                 :            : } PMSUBPROC_FLAGS;
     439                 :            : 
     440                 :            : 
     441                 :            : 
     442                 :            : typedef struct pmsubproc
     443                 :            : {
     444                 :            :         pid_t           pid;                    /* process id (0 when not running) */
     445                 :            :         PMSubType   procType;       /* process type */
     446                 :            :         PMSubStartCallback *serverStart; /* server start function */
     447                 :            :         char       *procName;
     448                 :            :         int        flags;          /* flags indicating in which kind
     449                 :            :                                                             * of instance to start the process */
     450                 :            :         bool        cleanupBackend; /* flag if process failure should
     451                 :            :                                                                 * cause cleanup of backend processes
     452                 :            :                                                                 * false = do not cleanup (eg. for gpperfmon) */
     453                 :            : } PMSubProc;
     454                 :            : 
     455                 :            : static PMSubProc PMSubProcList[MaxPMSubType] =

     742                 :            : static void SetBGWriterPID(pid_t pid)

     756                 :            : isFullPostmasterAndDatabaseIsAllowed(void)

     785                 :          0 :         }

     787                 :          0 :                 updateSegmentState(shutdownState, FaultTypeNotInitialized);

     789                 :            :

     793                 :          0 :         }

     796                 :            : /*
     797                 :            :  * Postmaster main entry point

     799                 :            : int
     800                 :            : PostmasterMain(int argc, char *argv[])
     801                 :            : {
     802                 :            :         int                     opt;
     803                 :            :         int                     status;
     804                 :         54 :         char       *userDoption = NULL;
     805                 :            :         int                     i;
     806                 :            :         char            stack_base;
     807                 :            : 
     808                 :            :         COMPILE_ASSERT(ARRAY_SIZE(gPmStateLabels) == PM__ENUMERATION_COUNT);
     809                 :            : 
     810                 :         54 :         MyProcPid = PostmasterPid = getpid();
     811                 :            : 
     812                 :         54 :         MyStartTime = time(NULL);
     813                 :            : 
     814                 :         54 :         IsPostmasterEnvironment = true;
     815                 :            : 
     816                 :            :         /* Set up reference point for stack depth checking */
     817                 :         54 :         stack_base_ptr = &stack_base;
     818                 :            : 
     819                 :            :         /*
     820                 :            :          * for security, no dir or file created can be group or other accessible
     821                 :            :          */
     822                 :         54 :         umask((mode_t) 0077);
     823                 :            : 
     824                 :            :         /*
     825                 :            :          * Fire up essential subsystems: memory management
     826                 :            :          */
     827                 :         54 :         MemoryContextInit();
     828                 :            : 
     829                 :            :         /*
     830                 :            :          * By default, palloc() requests in the postmaster will be allocated in
     831                 :            :          * the PostmasterContext, which is space that can be recycled by backends.
     832                 :            :          * Allocated data that needs to be available to backends should be
     833                 :            :          * allocated in TopMemoryContext.
     834                 :            :          */
     835                 :         54 :         PostmasterContext = AllocSetContextCreate(TopMemoryContext,
     836                 :            :                                                                                           "Postmaster",
     837                 :            :                                                                                           ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_MINSIZE,
     838                 :            :                                                                                           ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_INITSIZE,
     839                 :            :                                                                                           ALLOCSET_DEFAULT_MAXSIZE);
     840                 :         54 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(PostmasterContext);
     841                 :            :

     843                 :         54 :         getInstallationPaths(argv[0]);
     844                 :            : 
     845                 :            :         /*
     846                 :            :          * Options setup

     848                 :         54 :         InitializeGUCOptions();
     849                 :            : 
     850                 :         54 :         opterr = 1;
     851                 :            : 
     852                 :            :         /*
     853                 :            :          * Parse command-line options.  CAUTION: keep this in sync with
     854                 :            :          * tcop/postgres.c (the option sets should not conflict) and with the
     855                 :            :          * common help() function in main/main.c.
     856                 :            :          */
     857         [ +  + ]:        486 :         while ((opt = getopt(argc, argv, "A:B:bc:D:d:EeFf:h:ijk:lN:mM:nOo:Pp:r:S:sTt:UW:yx:-:")) != -1)
     858                 :            :         {
     859   [ -  -  -  +  :        432 :                 switch (opt)
          -  -  -  -  -  
          -  +  -  -  -  
          -  +  -  -  -  
          -  -  +  -  -  
          -  -  -  -  -  
             -  +  -  -  
                      - ]
     860                 :            :                 {
     861                 :            :                         case 'A':
     862                 :          0 :                                 SetConfigOption("debug_assertions", optarg, PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ARGV);
     863                 :          0 :                                 break;
     864                 :            : 
     865                 :            :                         case 'B':
     866                 :          0 :                                 SetConfigOption("shared_buffers", optarg, PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ARGV);
     867                 :          0 :                                 break;
     868                 :            : 
     869                 :            :                         case 'b':
     870                 :            :                                 /* Undocumented flag used for binary upgrades */
     871                 :          0 :                                 IsBinaryUpgrade = true;
     872                 :          0 :                                 break;
     873                 :            : 
     874                 :            :                         case 'D':
     875                 :         54 :                                 userDoption = optarg;
     876                 :         54 :                                 break;
     877                 :            : 
     878                 :            :                         case 'd':
     879                 :          0 :                                 set_debug_options(atoi(optarg), PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ARGV);
     880                 :          0 :                                 break;
     881                 :            : 
     882                 :            :                         /*
     883                 :            :                          * Normal PostgreSQL used 'E' flag to mean "log_statement='all'",
     884                 :            :                          * but we co-opted this letter for this... I'm afraid to change it,
     885                 :            :                          * because I don't know where this is used.
     886                 :            :              * ... it's used only here in postmaster.c; it causes the postmaster to
     887                 :            :              * fork a seqserver process.
     888                 :            :                          */
     889                 :            :                         case 'E':
     890                 :          0 :                                 Gp_entry_postmaster = true;

     894                 :          0 :                                 break;
     895                 :            : 
     896                 :            :                         case 'e':
     897                 :          0 :                                 SetConfigOption("datestyle", "euro", PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ARGV);
     898                 :          0 :                                 break;
     899                 :            : 
     900                 :            :                         case 'F':
     901                 :          0 :                                 SetConfigOption("fsync", "false", PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ARGV);
     902                 :          0 :                                 break;
     903                 :            : 
     904                 :            :                         case 'f':
     905         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (!set_plan_disabling_options(optarg, PGC_POSTMASTER, PGC_S_ARGV))
     906                 :            :                                 {
     907                 :          0 :                                         write_stderr("%s: invalid argument for option -f: \"%s\"\n",
     908                 :          0 :                                                                  progname, optarg);
     909                 :          0 :                                         ExitPostmaster(1);
     910                 :          0 :                                 }
     911                 :          0 :                                 break;
     912                 :            : 
     913                 :            :                         case 'h':

     915                 :          0 :                                 break;
     916                 :            :

     919                 :         54 :                                 break;
     920                 :            :

    1115                 :            :         /*
    1116                 :            :          * CDB/MPP/GPDB: Set the processor affinity (may be a no-op on
    1117                 :            :          * some platforms). The port number is nice to use because we know
    1118                 :            :          * that different segments on a single host will not have the same
    1119                 :            :          * port numbers.

    1126                 :          0 :                 setProcAffinity(PostPortNumber);

    1130                 :            : 
    1131                 :            :         /* And switch working directory into it */

    1133                 :            : 
    1134                 :            :         /*
    1135                 :            :      * CDB: Decouple NBuffers from MaxBackends.  The entry db doesn't benefit
    1136                 :            :      * from buffers in excess of the global catalog size; this is typically
    1137                 :            :      * small and unrelated to the number of clients.  Segment dbs need enough
    1138                 :            :      * buffers to accommodate the QEs concurrently accessing the database; but
    1139                 :            :      * non-leaf QEs don't necessarily access the database (some are used only
    1140                 :            :      * for sorting, hashing, etc); so again the number of buffers need not be
    1141                 :            :      * in proportion to the number of connections.
    1142                 :            :          */
    1143         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (NBuffers < 16)

    1148                 :            :                  */

    1150                 :          0 :                 ExitPostmaster(1);
    1151                 :          0 :         }

    1205                 :            :         /*
    1206                 :            :          * Now that we are done processing the postmaster arguments, reset
    1207                 :            :          * getopt(3) library so that it will work correctly in subprocesses.

    1214                 :            :         /* For debugging: display postmaster environment */

    1218                 :            : 
    1219         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 ereport(DEBUG3,
    1220                 :            :                                 (errmsg_internal("%s: PostmasterMain: initial environ dump:",
    1221                 :            :                                                                  progname)));
    1222         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 ereport(DEBUG3,
    1223                 :            :                          (errmsg_internal("-----------------------------------------")));
    1224         [ +  + ]:       1836 :                 for (p = environ; *p; ++p)
    1225         [ -  + ]:       1782 :                         ereport(DEBUG3,
    1226                 :            :                                         (errmsg_internal("\t%s", *p)));
    1227         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 ereport(DEBUG3,
    1228                 :            :                          (errmsg_internal("-----------------------------------------")));
    1229                 :            :         }
    1230                 :            : 
    1231                 :            :         /*
    1232                 :            :          * Fork away from controlling terminal, if silent_mode specified.
    1233                 :            :          *
    1234                 :            :          * Must do this before we grab any interlock files, else the interlocks
    1235                 :            :          * will show the wrong PID.
    1236                 :            :          */
    1237         [ +  - ]:         54 :         if (SilentMode)
    1238                 :         54 :                 pmdaemonize();
    1239                 :            : 
    1240                 :            :         /*
    1241                 :            :          * Create lockfile for data directory.
    1242                 :            :          *
    1243                 :            :          * We want to do this before we try to grab the input sockets, because the
    1244                 :            :          * data directory interlock is more reliable than the socket-file
    1245                 :            :          * interlock (thanks to whoever decided to put socket files in /tmp :-().
    1246                 :            :          * For the same reason, it's best to grab the TCP socket(s) before the
    1247                 :            :          * Unix socket.
    1248                 :            :          */
    1249                 :         54 :         CreateDataDirLockFile(true);
    1250                 :            : 
    1251                 :            :         /*
    1252                 :            :          * If timezone is not set, determine what the OS uses.  (In theory this
    1253                 :            :          * should be done during GUC initialization, but because it can take as
    1254                 :            :          * much as several seconds, we delay it until after we've created the
    1255                 :            :          * postmaster.pid file.  This prevents problems with boot scripts that
    1256                 :            :          * expect the pidfile to appear quickly.  Also, we avoid problems with
    1257                 :            :          * trying to locate the timezone files too early in initialization.)
    1258                 :            :          */
    1259                 :         54 :         pg_timezone_initialize();
    1260                 :            : 
    1261                 :            :         /*
    1262                 :            :          * Likewise, init timezone_abbreviations if not already set.
    1263                 :            :          */
    1264                 :         54 :         pg_timezone_abbrev_initialize();
    1265                 :            : 
    1266                 :            :         /*
    1267                 :            :          * Remember postmaster startup time
    1268                 :            :      * CDB: Moved this code up from below for use in error message headers.
    1269                 :            :          */
    1270                 :         54 :         PgStartTime = GetCurrentTimestamp();
    1271                 :            : 
    1272                 :            :         /*
    1273                 :            :          * Initialize SSL library, if specified.
    1274                 :            :          */

    1276         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (EnableSSL)
    1277                 :          0 :                 secure_initialize();

    1284                 :            :

    1315                 :            :

    1673                 :            : /*
    1674                 :            :  * Validate the proposed data directory

    1676                 :            : static void
    1677                 :            : checkDataDir(void)
    1678                 :            : {
    1679                 :            :         struct stat stat_buf;
    1680                 :            : 
    1681 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         54 :         Assert(DataDir);
    1682                 :            : 
    1683         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (stat(DataDir, &stat_buf) != 0)
    1684                 :            :         {
    1685         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (errno == ENOENT)
    1686         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(FATAL,
    1687                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
    1688                 :            :                                          errmsg("data directory \"%s\" does not exist",
    1689                 :            :                                                         DataDir)));
    1690                 :            :                 else
    1691         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(FATAL,
    1692                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
    1693                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not read permissions of directory \"%s\": %m",
    1694                 :            :                                                 DataDir)));
    1695                 :          0 :         }
    1696                 :            : 
    1697                 :            :         /* eventual chdir would fail anyway, but let's test ... */
    1698         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (!S_ISDIR(stat_buf.st_mode))
    1699         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(FATAL,
    1700                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
    1701                 :            :                                  errmsg("specified data directory \"%s\" is not a directory",
    1702                 :            :                                                 DataDir)));
    1703                 :            : 
    1704                 :            :         /*
    1705                 :            :          * Check that the directory belongs to my userid; if not, reject.
    1706                 :            :          *
    1707                 :            :          * This check is an essential part of the interlock that prevents two
    1708                 :            :          * postmasters from starting in the same directory (see CreateLockFile()).
    1709                 :            :          * Do not remove or weaken it.
    1710                 :            :          *
    1711                 :            :          * XXX can we safely enable this check on Windows?
    1712                 :            :          */
    1713                 :            : #if !defined(WIN32) && !defined(__CYGWIN__)
    1714         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (stat_buf.st_uid != geteuid())
    1715         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(FATAL,
    1716                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
    1717                 :            :                                  errmsg("data directory \"%s\" has wrong ownership",
    1718                 :            :                                                 DataDir),
    1719                 :            :                                  errhint("The server must be started by the user that owns the data directory.")));
    1720                 :            : #endif
    1721                 :            : 
    1722                 :            :         /*
    1723                 :            :          * Check if the directory has group or world access.  If so, reject.
    1724                 :            :          *
    1725                 :            :          * It would be possible to allow weaker constraints (for example, allow
    1726                 :            :          * group access) but we cannot make a general assumption that that is
    1727                 :            :          * okay; for example there are platforms where nearly all users
    1728                 :            :          * customarily belong to the same group.  Perhaps this test should be
    1729                 :            :          * configurable.
    1730                 :            :          *
    1731                 :            :          * XXX temporarily suppress check when on Windows, because there may not
    1732                 :            :          * be proper support for Unix-y file permissions.  Need to think of a
    1733                 :            :          * reasonable check to apply on Windows.
    1734                 :            :          */
    1735                 :            : #if !defined(WIN32) && !defined(__CYGWIN__)
    1736         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (stat_buf.st_mode & (S_IRWXG | S_IRWXO))
    1737         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(FATAL,
    1738                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
    1739                 :            :                                  errmsg("data directory \"%s\" has group or world access",
    1740                 :            :                                                 DataDir),
    1741                 :            :                                  errdetail("Permissions should be u=rwx (0700).")));
    1742                 :            : #endif
    1743                 :            : 
    1744                 :            :         /* Look for PG_VERSION before looking for pg_control */
    1745                 :         54 :         ValidatePgVersion(DataDir);
    1746                 :            : 
    1747                 :            :     /* for mirroring, the check for pg_control was removed.  a mirror must be able to start
    1748                 :            :      *  without a pg_control file.  The  check for pg_control will be done
    1749                 :            :      *  when the control file is read by xlog initialization.
    1750                 :            :      */
    1751                 :         54 : }

    1812                 :            : /*
    1813                 :            :  * This file is only created/used on primary and not on mirror
    1814                 :            :  * Hence .bak extension to filename, so that checkMirrorIntegrity tool skips checking it
    1815                 :            :  */
    1816                 :            : #define FTS_PROBE_FILE_NAME "/fts_probe_file.bak"
    1817                 :            : #define FTS_PROBE_MAGIC_STRING "FtS PrObEr MaGiC StRiNg, pRoBiNg cHeCk......."
    1818                 :            : 
    1819                 :            : /*
    1820                 :            :  * Check if we can smoothly read and write to data directory.
    1821                 :            :  *
    1822                 :            :  * O_DIRECT flag requires buffer to be OS/FS block aligned.
    1823                 :            :  * Best to have it IO Block alligned henece using BLCKSZ
    1824                 :            :  */
    1825                 :            : static bool 
    1826                 :            : checkIODataDirectory(void)
    1827                 :            : {
    1828 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         54 :         Assert(DataDir);
    1829                 :            : 
    1830                 :            :         int fd;
    1831                 :            :         char filename[MAXPGPATH];
    1832                 :         54 :         int size = BLCKSZ + BLCKSZ;
    1833                 :         54 :         int magic_len = strlen(FTS_PROBE_MAGIC_STRING) + 1;
    1834                 :         54 :         char *data = malloc(size);
    1835         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (data == NULL)
    1836                 :            :         {
    1837                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "FTS: Failed to allocate memory");
    1838                 :          0 :                 return true;
    1839                 :            :         }
    1840                 :         54 :         memset(data, 0, size);
    1841                 :            :         /*
    1842                 :            :          * Buffer needs to be alligned to BLOCK_SIZE for reads and writes if using O_DIRECT
    1843                 :            :          */
    1844                 :         54 :         char* dataAligned = (char *) TYPEALIGN(BLCKSZ, data);
    1845                 :            : 
    1846                 :         54 :         snprintf(filename, MAXPGPATH, "%s%s", DataDir, FTS_PROBE_FILE_NAME);
    1847                 :            : 
    1848                 :         54 :         errno = 0;
    1849                 :         54 :         bool failure = false;
    1850                 :            :         
    1851                 :         54 :         fd = BasicOpenFile(filename, O_RDWR | PG_O_DIRECT | O_EXCL,
    1852                 :            :                                            S_IRUSR | S_IWUSR);
    1853                 :         54 :         do
    1854                 :            :         {
    1855         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 if (fd < 0)
    1856                 :            :                 {
    1857         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (errno == ENOENT)
    1858                 :            :                         {
    1859                 :          0 :                                 elog(LOG, "FTS: \"%s\" file doesn't exist, creating it once.", filename);
    1860                 :          0 :                                 fd = BasicOpenFile(filename, O_RDWR | O_CREAT | O_EXCL,
    1861                 :            :                                            S_IRUSR | S_IWUSR);
    1862         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (fd < 0)
    1863                 :            :                                 {
    1864                 :          0 :                                         failure = true;
    1865         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         ereport(LOG, (errcode_for_file_access(),
    1866                 :            :                                                         errmsg("FTS: could not create file \"%s\": %m",
    1867                 :            :                                                                 filename)));
    1868                 :          0 :                                 }
    1869                 :            :                                 else
    1870                 :            :                                 {
    1871                 :          0 :                                         strncpy(dataAligned, FTS_PROBE_MAGIC_STRING, magic_len);
    1872         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         if (write(fd, dataAligned, BLCKSZ) != BLCKSZ)
    1873                 :            :                                         {
    1874         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                                 ereport(LOG, (errcode_for_file_access(), 
    1875                 :            :                                                                           errmsg("FTS: could not write file \"%s\" : %m", 
    1876                 :            :                                                                                          filename)));
    1877                 :          0 :                                                 failure = true;
    1878                 :          0 :                                         }
    1879                 :            :                                 }
    1880                 :          0 :                         }
    1881                 :            :                         else
    1882                 :            :                         {
    1883                 :            :                                 /*
    1884                 :            :                                  * Some other error
    1885                 :            :                                  */
    1886                 :          0 :                                 failure = true;
    1887         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(LOG, (errcode_for_file_access(), 
    1888                 :            :                                                 errmsg("FTS: could not open file \"%s\": %m", 
    1889                 :            :                                                         filename)));
    1890                 :            :                         }
    1891                 :          0 :                         break;
    1892                 :            :                 }
    1893                 :            : 
    1894                 :         54 :                 int len = read(fd, dataAligned, BLCKSZ);
    1895         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 if (len != BLCKSZ)
    1896                 :            :                 {
    1897         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(LOG, (errcode_for_file_access(), 
    1898                 :            :                                         errmsg("FTS: could not read file \"%s\" "
    1899                 :            :                                                 "(actual bytes read %d, required: %d): %m",
    1900                 :            :                                                 filename, len, BLCKSZ)));
    1901                 :          0 :                         failure = true;
    1902                 :          0 :                         break;
    1903                 :            :                 }
    1904                 :            : 
    1905         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 if (strncmp(dataAligned, FTS_PROBE_MAGIC_STRING, magic_len) != 0)
    1906                 :            :                 {
    1907         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(LOG, (errmsg("FTS: Read corrupted data from \"%s\" file", filename)));
    1908                 :          0 :                         failure = true;
    1909                 :          0 :                         break;
    1910                 :            :                 }
    1911                 :            : 
    1912         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 if (lseek(fd, (off_t) 0, SEEK_SET) < 0)
    1913                 :            :                 {
    1914         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(LOG, (errcode_for_file_access(),
    1915                 :            :                                         errmsg("FTS: could not seek in file \"%s\" to offset zero: %m",
    1916                 :            :                                         filename)));
    1917                 :          0 :                         failure = true;
    1918                 :          0 :                         break;
    1919                 :            :                 }
    1920                 :            : 
    1921                 :            :                 /*
    1922                 :            :                  * Read worked, lets overwrite what we read, to check if can write also
    1923                 :            :                  */
    1924         [ -  + ]:         54 :                 if (write(fd, dataAligned, BLCKSZ) != BLCKSZ)
    1925                 :            :                 {
    1926         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(LOG, (errcode_for_file_access(), 
    1927                 :            :                                         errmsg("FTS: could not write file \"%s\" : %m", 
    1928                 :            :                                         filename)));
    1929                 :          0 :                         failure = true;
    1930                 :          0 :                         break;
    1931                 :            :                 }
    1932                 :         54 :         } while (0);
    1933                 :            : 
    1934         [ +  - ]:         54 :         if (fd > 0)
    1935                 :            :         {
    1936                 :         54 :                 close(fd);
    1937                 :            : 
    1938                 :            :                 /*
    1939                 :            :                  * We are more concerned with IOs hanging than failures.
    1940                 :            :                  * Cleanup the file as detected the problem and reporting the same.
    1941                 :            :                  * This is done to cover for cases like:
    1942                 :            :                  * 1] FTS detects corruption/read failure on the file, reports to Master
    1943                 :            :                  * 2] Triggers failover to mirror
    1944                 :            :                  * 3] But if the file stays around, when it transitions back to Primary

    1947                 :            :                  */

    1949                 :            :                 {
    1950         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (unlink(filename) < 0)
    1951         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(LOG,
    1952                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    1953                 :            :                                 errmsg("could not unlink file \"%s\": %m", filename)));
    1954                 :          0 :                 }
    1955                 :         54 :         }
    1956                 :            : 
    1957         [ +  - ]:         54 :         if(data)
    1958                 :         54 :                 free(data);
    1959                 :            : 
    1960                 :         54 :         return failure;
    1961                 :         54 : }

    1963                 :            : #ifdef USE_TEST_UTILS
    1964                 :            : /*
    1965                 :            :  * Simulate an unexpected process exit using SimEx
    1966                 :            :  */
    1967                 :            : static void SimExProcExit()
    1968                 :            : {
    1969                 :            :         if (gp_simex_init && gp_simex_run && pmState == PM_RUN)
    1970                 :            :         {
    1971                 :            :                 pid_t pid = 0;
    1972                 :            :                 int sig = 0;
    1973                 :            :                 const char *procName = NULL;
    1974                 :            :                 const char *sigName = NULL;
    1975                 :            : 
    1976                 :            :                 if (gp_simex_class == SimExESClass_ProcKill)
    1977                 :            :                 {
    1978                 :            :                         sig = SIGKILL;
    1979                 :            :                         sigName = "SIGKILL";
    1980                 :            : 
    1981                 :            :                         SimExESSubClass subclass = SimEx_CheckInject();
    1982                 :            :                         if (subclass == SimExESSubClass_ProcKill_BgWriter)
    1983                 :            :                         {
    1984                 :            :                                 pid = BgWriterPID;
    1985                 :            :                                 procName = GetServerProcessTitle(pid);
    1986                 :            :                         }
    1987                 :            :                         else
    1988                 :            :                         {
    1989                 :            :                                 Assert(subclass == SimExESSubClass_OK &&
    1990                 :            :                                        "Unexpected ES subclass for SIGKILL injection");
    1991                 :            :                         }
    1992                 :            :                 }
    1993                 :            : 
    1994                 :            :                 if (pid != 0)
    1995                 :            :                 {
    1996                 :            :                         Assert(sig != 0);
    1997                 :            :                         Assert(procName != NULL);
    1998                 :            :                         Assert(sigName != NULL);
    1999                 :            : 
    2000                 :            :                         ereport(LOG,
    2001                 :            :                                         (errmsg_internal("sending %s to %s (%d)", sigName, procName, (int) pid)));
    2002                 :            :                         signal_child(pid, sig);
    2003                 :            :                 }
    2004                 :            :         }
    2005                 :            : }
    2006                 :            : #endif /* USE_TEST_UTILS */
    2007                 :            :

    2017                 :            : }

    2020                 :            : 
    2021                 :            : /*
    2022                 :            :  * Fork away from the controlling terminal (silent_mode option)
    2023                 :            :  *
    2024                 :            :  * Since this requires disconnecting from stdin/stdout/stderr (in case they're
    2025                 :            :  * linked to the terminal), we re-point stdin to /dev/null and stdout/stderr
    2026                 :            :  * to "pg_log/startup.log" from the data directory, where we're already chdir'd.
    2027                 :            :  */
    2028                 :            : static void
    2029                 :            : pmdaemonize(void)
    2030                 :            : {
    2031                 :            : #ifndef WIN32
    2032                 :         54 :         const char *pmlogname = "pg_log/startup.log";
    2033                 :            :         int                     dvnull;
    2034                 :            :         int                     pmlog;
    2035                 :            :         pid_t           pid;
    2036                 :            :         int                     res;
    2037                 :            : 
    2038                 :            :         /*
    2039                 :            :          * Make sure we can open the files we're going to redirect to.  If this
    2040                 :            :          * fails, we want to complain before disconnecting.  Mention the full path
    2041                 :            :          * of the logfile in the error message, even though we address it by
    2042                 :            :          * relative path.
    2043                 :            :          */
    2044                 :         54 :         dvnull = open(DEVNULL, O_RDONLY, 0);
    2045         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (dvnull < 0)
    2046                 :            :         {
    2047                 :          0 :                 write_stderr("%s: could not open file \"%s\": %s\n",
    2048                 :          0 :                                          progname, DEVNULL, strerror(errno));
    2049                 :          0 :                 ExitPostmaster(1);
    2050                 :          0 :         }
    2051                 :         54 :         pmlog = open(pmlogname, O_CREAT | O_WRONLY | O_APPEND, 0600);
    2052         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (pmlog < 0)
    2053                 :            :         {
    2054                 :          0 :                 write_stderr("%s: could not open log file \"%s/%s\": %s\n",
    2055                 :          0 :                                          progname, DataDir, pmlogname, strerror(errno));
    2056                 :          0 :                 ExitPostmaster(1);
    2057                 :          0 :         }
    2058                 :            : 
    2059                 :            :         /*
    2060                 :            :          * Okay to fork.
    2061                 :            :          */
    2062                 :         54 :         pid = fork_process();
    2063         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (pid == (pid_t) -1)
    2064                 :            :         {
    2065                 :          0 :                 write_stderr("%s: could not fork background process: %s\n",
    2066                 :          0 :                                          progname, strerror(errno));
    2067                 :          0 :                 ExitPostmaster(1);
    2068                 :          0 :         }
    2069         [ -  + ]:         54 :         else if (pid)
    2070                 :            :         {                                                       /* parent */
    2071                 :            :                 /* Parent should just exit, without doing any atexit cleanup */
    2072                 :          0 :                 _exit(0);
    2073                 :            :         }
    2074                 :            : 
    2075                 :         54 :         MyProcPid = PostmasterPid = getpid();           /* reset PID vars to child */
    2076                 :            : 
    2077                 :         54 :         MyStartTime = time(NULL);
    2078                 :            : 
    2079                 :            :         /*
    2080                 :            :          * Some systems use setsid() to dissociate from the TTY's process group,
    2081                 :            :          * while on others it depends on stdin/stdout/stderr.  Do both if possible.
    2082                 :            :          */
    2083                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_SETSID
    2084         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (setsid() < 0)
    2085                 :            :         {
    2086                 :          0 :                 write_stderr("%s: could not dissociate from controlling TTY: %s\n",
    2087                 :          0 :                                          progname, strerror(errno));
    2088                 :          0 :                 ExitPostmaster(1);
    2089                 :          0 :         }
    2090                 :            : #endif
    2091                 :            : 
    2092                 :            :         /*
    2093                 :            :          * Reassociate stdin/stdout/stderr.  fork_process() cleared any pending
    2094                 :            :          * output, so this should be safe.  The only plausible error is EINTR,
    2095                 :            :          * which just means we should retry.
    2096                 :            :          */
    2097                 :         54 :         do {
    2098                 :         54 :                 res = dup2(dvnull, 0);
    2099 [ -  + ][ -  + ]:         54 :         } while (res < 0 && errno == EINTR);
    2100                 :         54 :         close(dvnull);
    2101                 :         54 :         do {
    2102                 :         54 :                 res = dup2(pmlog, 1);
    2103 [ -  + ][ -  + ]:         54 :         } while (res < 0 && errno == EINTR);
    2104                 :         54 :         do {
    2105                 :         54 :                 res = dup2(pmlog, 2);
    2106 [ -  + ][ -  + ]:         54 :         } while (res < 0 && errno == EINTR);
    2107                 :         54 :         close(pmlog);
    2108                 :            : #else                                                   /* WIN32 */
    2109                 :            :         /* not supported */
    2110                 :            :         elog(FATAL, "silent_mode is not supported under Windows");
    2111                 :            : #endif   /* WIN32 */
    2112                 :         54 : }
    2113                 :            : 
    2114                 :            : static bool
    2115                 :            : ServiceStartable(PMSubProc *subProc)
    2116                 :            : {
    2117                 :            :         int flagNeeded;
    2118                 :            :         bool result;
    2119                 :            : 
    2120         [ -  + ]:       8424 :         if (Gp_entry_postmaster)
    2121                 :          0 :                 flagNeeded = PMSUBPROC_FLAG_QD;
    2122                 :            :         else
    2123                 :       8424 :                 flagNeeded = PMSUBPROC_FLAG_QE;
    2124                 :            : 
    2125                 :            :         /*
    2126                 :            :          * GUC gp_enable_gpperfmon controls the start
    2127                 :            :          * of both the 'perfmon' and 'stats sender' processes
    2128                 :            :          */
    2129 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:       8424 :         if ((subProc->procType == PerfmonProc || subProc->procType == PerfmonSegmentInfoProc)
    2130         [ +  + ]:       6750 :             && !gp_enable_gpperfmon)
    2131                 :       3348 :                 result = 0;
    2132                 :            :         else
    2133                 :       5076 :                 result = ((subProc->flags & flagNeeded) != 0);
    2134                 :            : 
    2135                 :       8424 :         return result;
    2136                 :            : }

    2138                 :            : /**
    2139                 :            :  * Reset pmState to PM_INIT and begin initialization sequence.
    2140                 :            :  */

    2142                 :            : RestartAfterPostmasterReset(void)
    2143                 :            : {
    2144                 :          0 :         pmState = PM_INIT;
    2145                 :          0 :         FatalError = false;
    2146                 :            : 
    2147                 :            :         /* done..serverLoop will process startup request */
    2148                 :          0 : }
    2149                 :            : 
    2150                 :            : static void
    2151                 :            : BeginResetOfPostmasterAfterChildrenAreShutDown(void)
    2152                 :            : {
    2153                 :          0 :         elog(LOG, "BeginResetOfPostmasterAfterChildrenAreShutDown: counter %d", pmResetCounter);
    2154                 :          0 :         pmResetCounter++;
    2155                 :            : 
    2156                 :            :         PrimaryMirrorMode pm_mode;
    2157                 :            :         SegmentState_e s_state;
    2158                 :            :         DataState_e d_state;

    2160                 :          0 :         getPrimaryMirrorStatusCodes(&pm_mode, &s_state, &d_state, &f_type);
    2161                 :            : 
    2162                 :            :         /* set flag indicating mirroring fault */
    2163                 :          0 :         bool hasFaultTypeMirror =
    2164 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         (pm_mode == PMModePrimarySegment && f_type == FaultTypeMirror) ||
    2165                 :          0 :                         getTransitionToFault();
    2166                 :          0 :         setFaultAfterReset(hasFaultTypeMirror);
    2167                 :            : 
    2168                 :            :         /* copy most recent transition request to local memory */
    2169                 :          0 :         copyTransitionParametersToLocalMemory();
    2170                 :            : 
    2171                 :            :         /*
    2172                 :            :          * Save the MPP session number from shared-memory before we reset
    2173                 :            :          * everything.  There is some risk here accessing the shared-memory,
    2174                 :            :          * but we don't want to reuse session-ids on the master.
    2175                 :            :          */
    2176         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (ProcGetMppLocalProcessCounter(&Save_MppLocalProcessCounter))
    2177         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
    2178                 :            :                                 (errmsg("gp_session_id high-water mark is %d",
    2179                 :            :                                                 Save_MppLocalProcessCounter)));
    2180                 :            : 
    2181                 :            :         /*
    2182                 :            :          * And, save the DTM start time, too.
    2183                 :            :          */
    2184                 :          0 :         Save_DtxStartTime = getDtxStartTime();
    2185                 :            : 
    2186                 :            :         /*
    2187                 :            :          * reset shared memory and semaphores;
    2188                 :            :          * this happens before forking the filerep peer reset process;
    2189                 :            :          * the latter process should not use information from shared memory;
    2190                 :            :          */
    2191                 :          0 :         shmem_exit(0 /*code*/);
    2192                 :          0 :         reset_shared(PostPortNumber, true /*isReset*/);
    2193                 :            : 
    2194                 :            :         /*
    2195                 :            :          * Remove old temporary files.  At this point there can be no other
    2196                 :            :          * Postgres processes running in this directory, so this should be safe.
    2197                 :            :          */
    2198                 :          0 :         RemovePgTempFiles();
    2199                 :            : 
    2200         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (primaryMirrorPostmasterResetShouldRestartPeer())
    2201                 :            :         {
    2202                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "BeginResetOfPostmasterAfterChildrenAreShutDown: should restart peer");
    2203                 :          0 :                 pmState = PM_POSTMASTER_RESET_FILEREP_PEER;
    2204                 :          0 :                 peerResetResult = PEER_RESET_NONE;
    2205                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "all server processes terminated; requested filerep peer reset");
    2206                 :          0 :                 FilerepPeerResetPID = StartFilerepPeerResetProcess();
    2207                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "peer reset process pid is %d", FilerepPeerResetPID);
    2208                 :          0 :         }
    2209                 :            :         else
    2210                 :            :         {
    2211         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
    2212                 :            :                         (errmsg("all server processes terminated; reinitializing")));
    2213                 :          0 :                 RestartAfterPostmasterReset();
    2214                 :            :         }
    2215                 :          0 : }
