    2218                 :            : /**
    2219                 :            :  * loop waiting for immediate shutdown to complete
    2220                 :            :  */
    2221                 :            : static void
    2222                 :            : ImmediateShutdownLoop(void)
    2223                 :            : {
    2224                 :          0 :     for ( ;; )
    2225                 :            :     {
    2226                 :          0 :         PG_SETMASK(&BlockSig);
    2227         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if ( need_call_reaper)
    2228                 :            :         {
    2229                 :          0 :             do_immediate_shutdown_reaper();
    2230                 :          0 :         }
    2231                 :            : 
    2232         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if ( FilerepPID == 0 )
    2233                 :            :         {
    2234                 :          0 :             break;
    2235                 :            :         }
    2236                 :            : 
    2237                 :          0 :         PG_SETMASK(&UnBlockSig);
    2238                 :          0 :         pg_usleep(100 * 1000L);
    2239                 :            :     }
    2240                 :            : 
    2241                 :          0 :     ExitPostmaster(0);
    2242                 :          0 : }
    2243                 :            : 
    2244                 :            : /*
    2245                 :            :  * Main idle loop of postmaster
    2246                 :            :  */
    2247                 :            : static int
    2248                 :            : ServerLoop(void)
    2249                 :            : {
    2250                 :            :         fd_set          readmask;
    2251                 :            :         int                     nSockets;
    2252                 :            :         time_t          now,
    2253                 :            :                                 last_touch_time;
    2254                 :            : 
    2255                 :         54 :         last_touch_time = time(NULL);
    2256                 :            : 
    2257                 :         54 :         nSockets = initMasks(&readmask);
    2258                 :            : 
    2259                 :         54 :     doRequestedPrimaryMirrorModeTransitions( Shutdown > NoShutdown );
    2260                 :            : 
    2261                 :       2538 :         for (;;)
    2262                 :            :         {
    2263                 :            :                 fd_set          rmask;
    2264                 :            :                 int                     selres;
    2265                 :            :                 int                     s;
    2266                 :            :         struct timeval timeout;
    2267                 :            : 
    2268                 :            :                 /*
    2269                 :            :                  * Wait for a connection request to arrive.
    2270                 :            :                  *
    2271                 :            :                  * We wait at most one minute, to ensure that the other background
    2272                 :            :                  * tasks handled below get done even when no requests are arriving.
    2273                 :            :                  *
    2274                 :            :                  * If we are in PM_WAIT_DEAD_END state, then we don't want to accept
    2275                 :            :                  * any new connections, so we don't call select() at all; just sleep
    2276                 :            :                  * for a little bit with signals unblocked.
    2277                 :            :                  */
    2278                 :       2592 :                 memcpy((char *) &rmask, (char *) &readmask, sizeof(fd_set));
    2279                 :            : 
    2280                 :       2592 :                 PG_SETMASK(&UnBlockSig);
    2281                 :            : 
    2282                 :            :                 /*
    2283                 :            :                  * we allow new connections in many cases (which could later be rejected because the database is shutting down,
    2284                 :            :                  *   but that gives a nicer message than potentially hanging the client's connection)
    2285                 :            :                  */
    2286                 :            :         bool acceptNewConnections;
    2287                 :            : 
    2288 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:       2592 :         if ( Shutdown == NoShutdown && FatalError)
    2289                 :            :         {
    2290                 :            :                 /** during postmaster reset, accept new connections to allow us to coordinate the reset */

    2600                 :            : 
    2601                 :            : /*
    2602                 :            :  * Read a client's startup packet and do something according to it.
    2603                 :            :  *
    2604                 :            :  * Returns STATUS_OK or STATUS_ERROR, or might call ereport(FATAL) and
    2605                 :            :  * not return at all.
    2606                 :            :  *
    2607                 :            :  * (Note that ereport(FATAL) stuff is sent to the client, so only use it
    2608                 :            :  * if that's what you want.  Return STATUS_ERROR if you don't want to
    2609                 :            :  * send anything to the client, which would typically be appropriate
    2610                 :            :  * if we detect a communications failure.)
    2611                 :            :  */
    2612                 :            : static int
    2613                 :            : ProcessStartupPacket(Port *port, bool SSLdone)
    2614                 :            : {
    2615                 :            :         int32           len;
    2616                 :            :         void       *buf;
    2617                 :            :         ProtocolVersion proto;
    2618                 :            :         MemoryContext oldcontext;
    2619                 :         54 :     char       *gpqeid = NULL;
    2620                 :            : 
    2621         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (pq_getbytes((char *) &len, 4) == EOF)
    2622                 :            :         {
    2623                 :            :                 /*
    2624                 :            :                  * EOF after SSLdone probably means the client didn't like our
    2625                 :            :                  * response to NEGOTIATE_SSL_CODE.      That's not an error condition, so
    2626                 :            :                  * don't clutter the log with a complaint.else if (strcmp(nameptr, "replication") == 0)
    2627                 :            :                         {
    2628                 :            :                                 if (!parse_bool(valptr, &am_walsender))
    2629                 :            :                                         ereport(FATAL,
    2630                 :            :                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
    2631                 :            :                                                          errmsg("invalid value for boolean option \"replication\"")));
    2632                 :            :                         }
    2633                 :            :                  */
    2634         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!SSLdone)
    2635         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(COMMERROR,
    2636                 :            :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_PROTOCOL_VIOLATION),
    2637                 :            :                                          errmsg("incomplete startup packet")));
    2638                 :          0 :                 return STATUS_ERROR;
    2639                 :            :         }
    2640                 :            : 
    2641                 :         54 :         len = ntohl(len);
    2642                 :         54 :         len -= 4;
    2643                 :            : 
    2644 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         54 :         if (len < (int32) sizeof(ProtocolVersion) ||
    2645                 :         54 :                 len > MAX_STARTUP_PACKET_LENGTH)
    2646                 :            :         {
    2647         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(COMMERROR,
    2648                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_PROTOCOL_VIOLATION),
    2649                 :            :                                  errmsg("invalid length of startup packet %ld",(long)len)));
    2650                 :          0 :                 return STATUS_ERROR;
    2651                 :            :         }
    2652                 :            : 
    2653                 :            :         /*
    2654                 :            :          * Allocate at least the size of an old-style startup packet, plus one
    2655                 :            :          * extra byte, and make sure all are zeroes.  This ensures we will have
    2656                 :            :          * null termination of all strings, in both fixed- and variable-length
    2657                 :            :          * packet layouts.
    2658                 :            :          */
    2659         [ +  - ]:         54 :         if (len <= (int32) sizeof(StartupPacket))
    2660                 :         54 :                 buf = palloc0(sizeof(StartupPacket) + 1);
    2661                 :            :         else
    2662                 :          0 :                 buf = palloc0(len + 1);
    2663                 :            : 
    2664         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (pq_getbytes(buf, len) == EOF)
    2665                 :            :         {
    2666         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(COMMERROR,
    2667                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_PROTOCOL_VIOLATION),
    2668                 :            :                                  errmsg("incomplete startup packet")));
    2669                 :          0 :                 return STATUS_ERROR;
    2670                 :            :         }
    2671                 :            : 
    2672                 :            :         /*
    2673                 :            :          * The first field is either a protocol version number or a special
    2674                 :            :          * request code.
    2675                 :            :          */

    2677                 :            :

    2690                 :          0 :                 return 127;

    2695         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (!disable_sig_alarm(false))

    2697                 :         54 :                 processPrimaryMirrorTransitionQuery(port, buf);
    2698                 :         54 :                 return 127;

    2723                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_socket_access(),
    2724                 :            :                                          errmsg("failed to send SSL negotiation response: %m")));
    2725                 :          0 :                         return STATUS_ERROR;    /* close the connection */
    2726                 :            :                 }

    2729 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (SSLok == 'S' && secure_open_server(port) == -1)

    2731                 :            : #endif

    2736                 :            : 
    2737                 :            :         /* Could add additional special packet types here */

    2739                 :            :         /*
    2740                 :            :          * Set FrontendProtocol now so that ereport() knows what format to send if

    2742                 :            :          */

    2744                 :            : 
    2745                 :            :         /* Check we can handle the protocol the frontend is using. */
    2746                 :            : 
    2747 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(proto) < PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(PG_PROTOCOL_EARLIEST) ||
    2748         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(proto) > PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(PG_PROTOCOL_LATEST) ||
    2749         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 (PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(proto) == PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(PG_PROTOCOL_LATEST) &&
    2750                 :          0 :                  PG_PROTOCOL_MINOR(proto) > PG_PROTOCOL_MINOR(PG_PROTOCOL_LATEST)))
    2751         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(FATAL,
    2752                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    2753                 :            :                                  errmsg("unsupported frontend protocol %u.%u: server supports %u.0 to %u.%u",
    2754                 :            :                                                 PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(proto), PG_PROTOCOL_MINOR(proto),
    2755                 :            :                                                 PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(PG_PROTOCOL_EARLIEST),
    2756                 :            :                                                 PG_PROTOCOL_MAJOR(PG_PROTOCOL_LATEST),
    2757                 :            :                                                 PG_PROTOCOL_MINOR(PG_PROTOCOL_LATEST))));

    2759                 :            :         /*
    2760                 :            :          * Now fetch parameters out of startup packet and save them into the Port

    2764                 :            :          * this storage on failure, since we aren't in the postmaster process

    2771                 :          0 :                 int32           offset = sizeof(ProtocolVersion);
    2772                 :            : 
    2773                 :            :                 /*

    2776                 :            :                  * zeroing extra byte above.

    2779                 :            : 
    2780         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 while (offset < len)
    2781                 :            :                 {
    2782                 :          0 :                         char       *nameptr = ((char *) buf) + offset;
    2783                 :            :                         int32           valoffset;
    2784                 :            :                         char       *valptr;
    2785                 :            : 
    2786         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (*nameptr == '\0')
    2787                 :          0 :                                 break;                  /* found packet terminator */
    2788                 :          0 :                         valoffset = offset + strlen(nameptr) + 1;

    2792                 :            : 
    2793         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (strcmp(nameptr, "database") == 0)

    2807                 :          0 :                         }

    2809                 :            :                         {

    2812                 :          0 :                                                                                         pstrdup(nameptr));

    2953                 :            : /**
    2954                 :            :  * Reads the next string up to a newline
    2955                 :            :  * if no string can be read then NULL is returned
    2956                 :            :  * otherwise a newly allocated, NULL-terminated string is returned
    2957                 :            :  */
    2958                 :            : static char *
    2959                 :            : readNextStringFromString(char *buf, int *offsetInOut, int length)
    2960                 :            : {
    2961                 :            :         char *result;
    2962                 :          0 :         int offset = *offsetInOut;
    2963                 :          0 :         int strEnd = offset;

    2965                 :            :         {
    2966                 :          0 :                 return NULL;
    2967                 :            :         }
    2968                 :            : 
    2969 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         while (strEnd < length && buf[strEnd] != '\n')
    2970                 :            :         {
    2971                 :          0 :                 strEnd++;
    2972                 :            :         }
    2973                 :          0 :         result = palloc(sizeof(char) * (strEnd - offset + 1));
    2974                 :          0 :         memcpy(result, buf + offset, strEnd - offset);
    2975                 :          0 :         result[strEnd - offset] = '\0';
    2976                 :            : 
    2977                 :            :         /* skip over newline */
    2978         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if ( strEnd < length)
    2979                 :          0 :                 strEnd++;
    2980                 :            : 
    2981                 :            :         /* result */
    2982                 :          0 :         *offsetInOut = strEnd;
    2983                 :          0 :         return result;
    2984                 :          0 : }

    2999                 :            :         }
    3000                 :            : 
    3001         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (wasRead)
    3002                 :          0 :                 *wasRead = true;
    3003                 :          0 :         res = atoi(val);
    3004                 :          0 :         pfree(val);
    3005                 :          0 :         return res;
    3006                 :          0 : }

    3035                 :          0 :         }

    3092                 :          0 :         }

    3099                 :          0 :         }

    3113                 :          0 :         sendPrimaryMirrorTransitionResult(statusBuf);

    3116                 :            : static void
    3117                 :            : processTransitionRequest_getMirrorStatus(void)
    3118                 :            : {
    3119                 :            :         /* this information is digested by gpstate so beware of changing its format */

    3121                 :            :

    3126                 :            : 
    3127                 :          0 :         getPrimaryMirrorStatusCodes(&pm_mode, &s_state, &d_state, &f_type);
    3128                 :            : 
    3129                 :          0 :         int64 resyncNumCompleted = FileRepResync_GetBlocksSynchronized();
    3130                 :          0 :         int64 resyncTotalToComplete = FileRepResync_GetTotalBlocksToSynchronize();
    3131                 :          0 :         int64 changeTrackingBytesUsed = ChangeTracking_GetTotalSpaceUsedOnDisk();
    3132                 :            : 
    3133                 :          0 :         int fsobjCount = FileRepResync_GetCurFsobjCount();
    3134                 :          0 :         int totalFsobjCount = FileRepResync_GetTotalFsobjCount();
    3135                 :            : 
    3136                 :            :         /**
    3137                 :            :          * Note: call this one AFTER calling the others above -- it will return a sentinel
    3138                 :            :          *       if estimates are invalid, and we want to be sure we get the sentinel
    3139                 :            :          *       (the system can go from valid to invalid estimates but no vice-versa)
    3140                 :            :          */

    3578                 :            : static void

    3591                 :            : 
    3592                 :         54 :         pq_endmessage(&buf);
    3593                 :         54 :         pq_flush();
    3594                 :         54 : }

    3596                 :            : /**

    3607                 :            :

    3616         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (length != 0)
    3617                 :            :         {
    3618         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(COMMERROR, (errcode(ERRCODE_PROTOCOL_VIOLATION),
    3619                 :            :                                  errmsg("corrupt length value %d in primary mirror query packet", length)));
    3620                 :          0 :                 return;
    3621                 :            :         }
    3622                 :            : 
    3623                 :         54 :         SIMPLE_FAULT_INJECTOR(SegmentProbeResponse);
    3624                 :            :

    3626                 :            : 
    3627                 :            :         /* check if resync has completed on both primary and mirror */
    3628 [ +  - ][ +  - ]:         54 :         if (pm_mode == PMModePrimarySegment && d_state == DataStateInSync && isResyncRunning())
                 [ -  + ]
    3629                 :            :         {
    3630                 :          0 :                 d_state = DataStateInResync;
    3631                 :          0 :         }
    3632                 :            : 
    3633                 :            :         /* check if segment is mirrorless primary */
    3634         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (pm_mode == PMModeMirrorlessSegment)
    3635                 :            :         {
    3636 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(d_state == DataStateNotInitialized);
    3637                 :            : 
    3638                 :            :                 /* report segment as in sync to conform with master's segment configuration */
    3639                 :          0 :                 d_state = DataStateInSync;
    3640                 :          0 :         }
    3641                 :            : 
    3642                 :            :         /* in case of a mirroring fault, check if there is a NIC failure */

                 [ #  # ]
    3644         [ -  + ]:         54 :              (pm_mode == PMModeMirrorSegment &&

    3648                 :            :                 {
    3649                 :            :                         /* report NIC failure to FTS */

    3651                 :          0 :                 }
    3652                 :          0 :         }
    3653                 :            :         
    3654         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (gp_log_fts >= GPVARS_VERBOSITY_VERBOSE)

    3658                 :          0 :         }
    3659                 :            : 
    3660                 :            :         /*
    3661                 :            :          * FTS apart from below code doesn't perform any disk IO,
    3662                 :            :          * hence adding code to perform reads and writes to segment data directory.
    3663                 :            :          * Several DU cases exposed FTS not detecting server hangs because of IO hang.

    3665                 :            :          * causing FTS to detect the problem.
    3666                 :            :          */

    3670                 :         54 :                         f_type == FaultTypeNotInitialized)

    3673         [ -  + ]:         54 :                         if (failure)
    3674                 :            :                         {
    3675                 :          0 :                                 elog(LOG, "FTS_PROBE for IO Check: FAILED");
    3676                 :          0 :                                 s_state = SegmentStateFault;
    3677                 :          0 :                                 f_type = FaultTypeIO; 
    3678                 :            : 
    3679                 :          0 :                                 FileRep_SetSegmentState(s_state, f_type);
    3680                 :          0 :                         }
    3681                 :         54 :                 }
    3682                 :         54 :         }
    3683                 :            : 
    3684                 :         54 :         sendPrimaryMirrorTransitionQuery((uint32)pm_mode, (uint32)s_state, (uint32)d_state, (uint32)f_type);
    3685                 :            : 
    3686                 :         54 :         return;
    3687                 :         54 : }

    3689                 :            : /*
    3690                 :            :  * The client has sent a cancel request packet, not a normal
    3691                 :            :  * start-a-new-connection packet.  Perform the necessary processing.
    3692                 :            :  * Nothing is sent back to the client.
    3693                 :            :  */
    3694                 :            : static void
    3695                 :            : processCancelRequest(Port *port, void *pkt, MsgType code)
    3696                 :            : {
    3697                 :          0 :         CancelRequestPacket *canc = (CancelRequestPacket *) pkt;
    3698                 :            :         int                     backendPID;
    3699                 :            :         long            cancelAuthCode;
    3700                 :            :         Backend    *bp;
    3701                 :            : 
    3702                 :            : #ifndef EXEC_BACKEND
    3703                 :            :         Dlelem     *curr;
    3704                 :            : #else
    3705                 :            :         int                     i;
    3706                 :            : #endif
    3707                 :            : 
    3708                 :          0 :         backendPID = (int) ntohl(canc->backendPID);
    3709                 :          0 :         cancelAuthCode = (long) ntohl(canc->cancelAuthCode);
    3710                 :            : 
    3711                 :            :         /*
    3712                 :            :          * See if we have a matching backend.  In the EXEC_BACKEND case, we can no
    3713                 :            :          * longer access the postmaster's own backend list, and must rely on the
    3714                 :            :          * duplicate array in shared memory.
    3715                 :            :          */

    3717         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (curr = DLGetHead(BackendList); curr; curr = DLGetSucc(curr))
    3718                 :            :         {
    3719                 :          0 :                 bp = (Backend *) DLE_VAL(curr);
    3720                 :            : #else
    3721                 :            :         for (i = MaxLivePostmasterChildren() - 1; i >= 0; i--)
    3722                 :            :         {
    3723                 :            :                 bp = (Backend *) &ShmemBackendArray[i];
    3724                 :            : #endif
    3725         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (bp->pid == backendPID)
    3726                 :            :                 {
    3727         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (bp->cancel_key == cancelAuthCode)
    3728                 :            :                         {
    3729                 :            :                                 /* Found a match; signal that backend to cancel current op */
    3730         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 if (code == FINISH_REQUEST_CODE)
    3731                 :            :                                 {
    3732         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         ereport(LOG,
    3733                 :            :                                                         (errmsg_internal("query finish request to process %d",
    3734                 :            :                                                                                          backendPID)));
    3735                 :          0 :                                         SendProcSignal(bp->pid, PROCSIG_QUERY_FINISH,
    3736                 :            :                                                                    InvalidBackendId);
    3737                 :          0 :                                 }
    3738                 :            :                                 else
    3739                 :            :                                 {
    3740         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                         ereport(DEBUG2,
    3741                 :            :                                                         (errmsg_internal("processing cancel request: sending SIGINT to process %d",
    3742                 :            :                                                                                          backendPID)));
    3743                 :          0 :                                         signal_child(bp->pid, SIGINT);
    3744                 :            :                                 }
    3745                 :          0 :                         }
    3746                 :            :                         else
    3747                 :            :                                 /* Right PID, wrong key: no way, Jose */
    3748         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport(LOG,
    3749                 :            :                                                 (errmsg("wrong key in cancel request for process %d",
    3750                 :            :                                                                 backendPID)));
    3751                 :          0 :                         return;
    3752                 :            :                 }
    3753                 :          0 :         }
    3754                 :            : 
    3755                 :            :         /* No matching backend */
    3756         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ereport(LOG,
    3757                 :            :                         (errmsg("PID %d in cancel request did not match any process",
    3758                 :            :                                         backendPID)));
    3759                 :          0 : }

    3761                 :            : /*
    3762                 :            :  * canAcceptConnections --- check to see if database state allows connections.
    3763                 :            :  */
    3764                 :            : static enum CAC_state
    3765                 :            : canAcceptConnections(void)
    3766                 :            : {
    3767                 :            :         /* Can't start backends when in startup/shutdown/recovery state. */
    3768         [ +  + ]:        891 :         if (pmState != PM_RUN)
    3769                 :            :         {
    3770                 :            :                 PrimaryMirrorMode mirrorMode;
    3771                 :            : 
    3772         [ -  + ]:        108 :                 if (Shutdown > NoShutdown)
    3773                 :          0 :                         return CAC_SHUTDOWN;    /* shutdown is pending */
    3774                 :            : 
    3775                 :            :                 /*
    3776                 :            :                  * check to see if we are a quiescent or a mirror.
    3777                 :            :                  * We want to tell the client that we are in quiescent mode
    3778                 :            :                  * before checking startup or not, because this mode is
    3779                 :            :                  * always under startup state.  However, for mirror mode
    3780                 :            :                  * we first check whether we are in startup, as that's important
    3781                 :            :                  * in other cases.
    3782                 :            :                  */
    3783         [ +  - ]:        108 :                 if (isQuiescentMode(&mirrorMode))
    3784                 :        108 :                         return CAC_MIRROR_OR_QUIESCENT;
    3785                 :            : 
    3786 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (!FatalError &&
    3787         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         (pmState == PM_STARTUP ||
    3788         [ #  # ]:          0 :                          pmState == PM_STARTUP_PASS2 ||
    3789         [ #  # ]:          0 :                          pmState == PM_STARTUP_PASS3 ||
    3790         [ #  # ]:          0 :                          pmState == PM_STARTUP_PASS4 ||
    3791         [ #  # ]:          0 :                          pmState == PM_RECOVERY ||
    3792                 :          0 :                          pmState == PM_RECOVERY_CONSISTENT))
    3793                 :          0 :                         return CAC_STARTUP; /* normal startup */
    3794                 :            : 
    3795         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (mirrorMode == PMModeMirrorSegment)
    3796                 :          0 :                         return CAC_MIRROR_OR_QUIESCENT;
    3797                 :            : 
    3798                 :          0 :                 return CAC_RECOVERY;    /* else must be crash recovery */
    3799                 :            :         }
    3800                 :            : 
    3801                 :            :         /*
    3802                 :            :          * Don't start too many children.
    3803                 :            :          *
    3804                 :            :          * We allow more connections than we can have backends here because some
    3805                 :            :          * might still be authenticating; they might fail auth, or some existing
    3806                 :            :          * backend might exit before the auth cycle is completed. The exact
    3807                 :            :          * MaxBackends limit is enforced when a new backend tries to join the
    3808                 :            :          * shared-inval backend array.
    3809                 :            :          *

    3812                 :            :          */

    3814                 :          0 :                 return CAC_TOOMANY;
    3815                 :            : 
    3816                 :        783 :         return CAC_OK;
    3817                 :        891 : }

    3820                 :            : /*
    3821                 :            :  * ConnCreate -- create a local connection data structure

    3823                 :            : static Port *

    3831                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OUT_OF_MEMORY),
    3832                 :            :                                  errmsg("out of memory")));
    3833                 :          0 :                 ExitPostmaster(1);
    3834                 :          0 :         }
    3835                 :            : 
    3836         [ -  + ]:        891 :         if (StreamConnection(serverFd, port) != STATUS_OK)
    3837                 :            :         {

    3890                 :            : /*
    3891                 :            :  * ClosePostmasterPorts -- close all the postmaster's open sockets
    3892                 :            :  *
    3893                 :            :  * This is called during child process startup to release file descriptors
    3894                 :            :  * that are not needed by that child process.  The postmaster still has
    3895                 :            :  * them open, of course.
    3896                 :            :  *
    3897                 :            :  * Note: we pass am_syslogger as a boolean because we don't want to set
    3898                 :            :  * the global variable yet when this is called.
    3899                 :            :  */
    3900                 :            : void
    3901                 :            : ClosePostmasterPorts(bool am_syslogger)
    3902                 :            : {
    3903                 :            :         int                     i;
    3904                 :            : 
    3905                 :            :         /* Close the listen sockets */
    3906         [ +  + ]:       3510 :         for (i = 0; i < MAXLISTEN; i++)
    3907                 :            :         {
    3908         [ +  + ]:       3456 :                 if (ListenSocket[i] != -1)
    3909                 :            :                 {
    3910                 :        162 :                         StreamClose(ListenSocket[i]);
    3911                 :        162 :                         ListenSocket[i] = -1;
    3912                 :        162 :                 }
    3913                 :       3456 :         }
    3914                 :            : 
    3915                 :            :         /* If using syslogger, close the read side of the pipe */
    3916         [ +  - ]:         54 :         if (!am_syslogger)
    3917                 :            :         {
    3918                 :            : #ifndef WIN32
    3919         [ +  - ]:         54 :                 if (syslogPipe[0] >= 0)

    3923                 :            :                 if (syslogPipe[0])
    3924                 :            :                         CloseHandle(syslogPipe[0]);
    3925                 :            :                 syslogPipe[0] = 0;
    3926                 :            : #endif
    3927                 :         54 :         }
    3928                 :         54 : }

    3931                 :            : /*
    3932                 :            :  * reset_shared -- reset shared memory and semaphores

    3935                 :            : static void

    3937                 :            : {
    3938         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (isReset)
    3939                 :            :         {

    3949                 :            :

    3952                 :            :          *
    3953                 :            :          * Note: in each "cycle of life" we will normally assign the same IPC keys
    3954                 :            :          * (if using SysV shmem and/or semas), since the port number is used to
    3955                 :            :          * determine IPC keys.  This helps ensure that we will clean up dead IPC

    3957                 :            :          */

    3967                 :            :         }

    3970                 :            : /*
    3971                 :            :  * SIGHUP -- reread config files, and tell children to do same

    4029                 :            : /*
    4030                 :            :  * pmdie -- signal handler for processing various postmaster signals.
    4031                 :            :  */
    4032                 :            : static void
    4033                 :            : pmdie(SIGNAL_ARGS)
    4034                 :            : {
    4035                 :          0 :         int                     save_errno = errno;
    4036                 :            : 
    4037                 :          0 :         PG_SETMASK(&BlockSig);
    4038                 :            : 
    4039         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ereport(DEBUG2,
    4040                 :            :                         (errmsg_internal("postmaster received signal %d",
    4041                 :            :                                                          postgres_signal_arg)));
    4042                 :            : 
    4043   [ #  #  #  # ]:          0 :         switch (postgres_signal_arg)
    4044                 :            :         {
    4045                 :            :                 case SIGTERM:
    4046                 :            : 
    4047                 :            :                         /*
    4048                 :            :                          * Smart Shutdown:
    4049                 :            :                          *
    4050                 :            :                          * Wait for children to end their work, then shut down.
    4051                 :            :                          */
    4052         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (Shutdown >= SmartShutdown)
    4053                 :          0 :                                 break;
    4054                 :          0 :                         Shutdown = SmartShutdown;
    4055         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(LOG,
    4056                 :            :                                         (errmsg("received smart shutdown request"),
    4057                 :            :                                          errSendAlert(true)));
    4058                 :            : 
    4059                 :          0 :                         need_call_reaper = true;
    4060         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if ( pmState < PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN)
    4061                 :          0 :                             pmState = PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN;
    4062                 :          0 :                         signal_child_if_up(FilerepPeerResetPID, SIGQUIT);
    4063                 :          0 :                         break;
    4064                 :            : 
    4065                 :            :                 case SIGINT:
    4066                 :            : 
    4067                 :            :                         /*
    4068                 :            :                          * Fast Shutdown:
    4069                 :            :                          *
    4070                 :            :                          * Abort all children with SIGTERM (rollback active transactions
    4071                 :            :                          * and exit) and shut down when they are gone.
    4072                 :            :                          */
    4073         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (Shutdown >= FastShutdown)
    4074                 :          0 :                                 break;
    4075                 :          0 :                         Shutdown = FastShutdown;
    4076         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(LOG,
    4077                 :            :                                         (errmsg("received fast shutdown request"),
    4078                 :            :                                          errSendAlert(true)));
    4079                 :            : 
    4080                 :          0 :                         need_call_reaper = true;
    4081         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if ( pmState < PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN)
    4082                 :          0 :                             pmState = PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN;
    4083                 :            :             else
    4084                 :            :             {
    4085                 :            :                 /* we were already stopping children ... rerun the signals for the current state to
    4086                 :            :                  *   ensure that the correct signal level is applied */
    4087                 :          0 :                 pmStateMachineMustRunActions = true;
    4088                 :            :             }
    4089                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(FilerepPeerResetPID, SIGQUIT);
    4090                 :          0 :             break;
    4091                 :            : 
    4092                 :            :                 case SIGQUIT:
    4093                 :            : 
    4094                 :            :                         /*
    4095                 :            :                          * Immediate Shutdown:
    4096                 :            :                          *
    4097                 :            :                          * abort all children with SIGQUIT and exit without attempt to
    4098                 :            :                          * properly shut down data base system.
    4099                 :            :                          */
    4100         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(LOG,
    4101                 :            :                                         (errmsg("received immediate shutdown request"),
    4102                 :            :                                      errSendAlert(true)));
    4103                 :            : 
    4104                 :          0 :             signal_filerep_to_shutdown(SegmentStateImmediateShutdown);
    4105                 :            : 
    4106                 :          0 :                         SignalChildren(SIGQUIT);
    4107                 :          0 :                         signal_child_if_up(StartupPID, SIGQUIT);
    4108                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(StartupPass2PID, SIGQUIT);
    4109                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(StartupPass3PID, SIGQUIT);
    4110                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(StartupPass4PID, SIGQUIT);
    4111                 :          0 :                         StopServices(0, SIGQUIT);
    4112                 :          0 :                         signal_child_if_up(BgWriterPID, SIGQUIT);
    4113                 :          0 :                         signal_child_if_up(CheckpointerPID, SIGQUIT);
    4114                 :          0 :                         signal_child_if_up(WalWriterPID, SIGQUIT);
    4115                 :          0 :                         signal_child_if_up(WalReceiverPID, SIGQUIT);
    4116                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(AutoVacPID, SIGQUIT);
    4117                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(PgArchPID, SIGQUIT);
    4118                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(PgStatPID, SIGQUIT);
    4119                 :          0 :             signal_child_if_up(FilerepPeerResetPID, SIGQUIT);
    4120                 :            : 
    4121                 :            :             /* if you add more processes here then also update do_immediate_shutdown_reaper */
    4122                 :            : 
    4123                 :          0 :             Shutdown = ImmediateShutdown;
    4124                 :          0 :                         break;
    4125                 :            :         }
    4126                 :            : 
    4127         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Debug_print_server_processes)
    4128                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG,"returning from pmdie");
    4129                 :            : 
    4130                 :          0 :         PG_SETMASK(&UnBlockSig);
    4131                 :            : 
    4132                 :          0 :         errno = save_errno;
    4133                 :          0 : }

    4135                 :            : static void
    4136                 :            : zeroIfPidEqual(int pid, pid_t *ptrToPidToCheckAndZero)
    4137                 :            : {
    4138         [ #  # ]:          0 :     if ( pid == *ptrToPidToCheckAndZero)
    4139                 :            :     {

    4143                 :            : 
    4144                 :            : /* These macros hide platform variations in getting child status.
    4145                 :            :  * They are used by do_reaper and do_immediate_shutdown_reaper
    4146                 :            :  */
    4147                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_WAITPID
    4148                 :            :         int                     status;                 /* child exit status */
    4149                 :            : 
    4150                 :            : #define REAPER_LOOPTEST()               ((pid = waitpid(-1, &status, WNOHANG)) > 0)
    4151                 :            : #define REAPER_LOOPHEADER()     (exitstatus = status)
    4152                 :            : #else                                                   /* !HAVE_WAITPID */
    4153                 :            : #ifndef WIN32
    4154                 :            :         union wait      status;                 /* child exit status */
    4155                 :            : 
    4156                 :            : #define REAPER_LOOPTEST()               ((pid = wait3(&status, WNOHANG, NULL)) > 0)
    4157                 :            : #define REAPER_LOOPHEADER()     (exitstatus = status.w_status)
    4158                 :            : #else                                                   /* WIN32 */
    4159                 :            : #define REAPER_LOOPTEST()               ((pid = win32_waitpid(&exitstatus)) > 0)
    4160                 :            : #define REAPER_LOOPHEADER()
    4161                 :            : #endif   /* WIN32 */
    4162                 :            : #endif   /* HAVE_WAITPID */
    4163                 :            : 
    4164                 :            : 
    4165                 :            : /**
    4166                 :            :  * Check for dead processes during immediate shutdown

    4169                 :            : do_immediate_shutdown_reaper(void)

    4173                 :            :         int                     exitstatus;             /* its exit status */

    4175                 :          0 :         need_call_reaper = 0;
    4176                 :            : 
    4177         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ereport(DEBUG4, (errmsg_internal("reaping dead processes")));
    4178                 :            : 
    4179         [ #  # ]:          0 :         while (REAPER_LOOPTEST())
    4180                 :            :         {
    4181                 :          0 :                 REAPER_LOOPHEADER();
    4182                 :            : 
    4183                 :          0 :         zeroIfPidEqual(pid, &FilerepPID);
    4184                 :            : 
    4185         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (Dlelem *curr = DLGetHead(BackendList); curr; curr = DLGetSucc(curr))

    4200                 :          0 :         }
    4201                 :            : 
    4202                 :          0 :         zeroIfPidEqual(pid, &BgWriterPID);
    4203                 :          0 :         zeroIfPidEqual(pid, &CheckpointerPID);

    4205                 :          0 :                 zeroIfPidEqual(pid, &WalWriterPID);
    4206                 :          0 :         zeroIfPidEqual(pid, &AutoVacPID);

    4208                 :          0 :         zeroIfPidEqual(pid, &PgStatPID);

    4244                 :            :

    4261                 :            :         {
    4262 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:         54 :                 if (XLogArchivingActive() && PgArchPID == 0)

    4377                 :            :                          * Unexpected exit of startup process (including FATAL exit)
    4378                 :            :                          * during PM_STARTUP is treated as catastrophic. There are no
    4379                 :            :                          * other processes running yet, so we can just exit.
    4380                 :            :                          */

    4382                 :            :                         {

    4384                 :          0 :                                                          pid, exitstatus);

    4386                 :            :                                                 (errmsg("aborting startup due to startup process failure")));

    4388                 :          0 :                         }
    4389                 :            : 
    4390                 :            :                         /*
    4391                 :            :                          * Startup process exited in response to a shutdown request (or it
    4392                 :            :                          * completed normally regardless of the shutdown request).
    4393                 :            :                          */

    4396                 :            :                         {

    4398                 :          0 :                                 continue;
    4399                 :            :                         }
    4400                 :            : 
    4401                 :            :                         /*
    4402                 :            :                          * Any unexpected exit (including FATAL exit) of the startup
    4403                 :            :                          * process is treated as a crash, except that we don't want to
    4404                 :            :                          * reinitialize.
    4405                 :            :                          */
    4406         [ -  + ]:         54 :                         if (!EXIT_STATUS_0(exitstatus))

    4410                 :          0 :                                 continue;
    4411                 :            :                         }
    4412                 :            : 
    4413                 :            :                         /*
    4414                 :            :                          * When we are not doing single backend startup, we do
    4415                 :            :                          * multiple startup passes.
    4416                 :            :                          *
    4417                 :            :                          * If Crash Recovery is needed we do Pass 2 and 3.
    4418                 :            :                          *

    4420                 :            :                          * well as after Crash Recovery.
    4421                 :            :                          */

    4424 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                             Assert(StartupPass2PID == 0);
    4425                 :          0 :                                 StartupPass2PID = StartupPass2DataBase();

    4427                 :            : 
    4428                 :          0 :                                 pmState = PM_STARTUP_PASS2;
    4429                 :          0 :                         }
    4430         [ +  - ]:         54 :                         else if (XLogStartupIntegrityCheckNeeded())
    4431                 :            :                         {
    4432                 :            :                                 /*
    4433                 :            :                                  * Clean shutdown case and wants (and can do) integrity checks.
    4434                 :            :                                  */
    4435 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         54 :                             Assert(StartupPass4PID == 0);
    4436                 :         54 :                                 StartupPass4PID = StartupPass4DataBase();
    4437 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:         54 :                                 Assert(StartupPass4PID != 0);
    4438                 :            :

    4440                 :         54 :                         }

    4442                 :            :                         {
    4443                 :            :                                 /*
    4444                 :            :                                  * Startup succeeded, commence normal operations
    4445                 :            :                                  */

    4447                 :          0 :                                         didServiceProcessWork &= true;
    4448                 :            :                         }
    4449                 :            : 
    4450                 :         54 :                         continue;
    4451                 :            :                 }
    4452                 :            : 
    4453                 :            :                 /*
    4454                 :            :                  * Check if this child was a startup pass 2 process.
    4455                 :            :                  */
    4456         [ -  + ]:        891 :                 if (pid == StartupPass2PID)
    4457                 :            :                 {
    4458                 :          0 :                         StartupPass2PID = 0;
    4459                 :            : 
    4460 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(isPrimaryMirrorModeAFullPostmaster(true));
    4461                 :            : 
    4462                 :            :                         /*
    4463                 :            :                          * Unexpected exit of startup process (including FATAL exit)
    4464                 :            :                          * during PM_STARTUP is treated as catastrophic. There are no
    4465                 :            :                          * other processes running yet, so we can just exit.
    4466                 :            :                          */
    4467 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (pmState == PM_STARTUP_PASS2 && !EXIT_STATUS_0(exitstatus))

    4623                 :            : 
    4624                 :            :                         /*
    4625                 :            :                          * Startup Pass 4 can perform PersistentTable-Catalog verification
    4626                 :            :                          * too. But, it is turned on when appropriate GUC is set.
    4627                 :            :                          *
    4628                 :            :                          * If the GUC is set --
    4629                 :            :                          * 1. Both Non-DB specific and DB-specific verification checks
    4630                 :            :                          * are executed to see if the system is consistent.
    4631                 :            :                          * 2. First run of Pass 4 performs basic integrity checks and
    4632                 :            :                          * non-DB specific checks. At the same time, next DB on which DB-specific
    4633                 :            :                          * verifications are to be performed is selected.
    4634                 :            :                          * 3. This DB selected in #2 will be used in the next cycle of Pass 4
    4635                 :            :                          * as a new spawned process for verification purposes. At the same time,
    4636                 :            :                          * a new database is selected for the subsequent cycle and thus this
    4637                 :            :                          * continues until there are no more DBs left to be verified in the system.
    4638                 :            :                          */

    4794                 :            :                                 /*
    4795                 :            :                                  * If filerep main process dies unexpectedly, we should reset
    4796                 :            :                                  * the shared memory, otherwise filerep subprocesses cannot
    4797                 :            :                                  * revive.  Because they are still connected to shared memory
    4798                 :            :                                  * and can change segment status etc in case mirror starts
    4799                 :            :                                  * the peer reset, we should tell them to die immediately
    4800                 :            :                                  * before running reset.  Since this is an immediate shutdown
    4801                 :            :                                  * request, we don't have a way to wait for the completion.

    4888                 :          0 :                         continue;

    4992                 :            : 
    4993                 :            :                 /*
    4994                 :            :                  * Wait for all important children to exit, then reset shmem and

    5015                 :            : 
    5016                 :            :                 /*
    5017                 :            :                  * Start waiting for dead_end children to die. This state change causes

    5026                 :          0 :                         goto reaper_done;

    5165                 :            : /*
    5166                 :            :  * CleanupBackend -- cleanup after terminated backend.
    5167                 :            :  *
    5168                 :            :  * Remove all local state associated with backend.
    5169                 :            :  */
    5170                 :            : static void
    5171                 :            : CleanupBackend(int pid,
    5172                 :            :                            int exitstatus)      /* child's exit status. */
    5173                 :            : {
    5174                 :            :         Dlelem     *curr;
    5175                 :            : 
    5176                 :        837 :         LogChildExit(DEBUG2, _("server process"), pid, exitstatus);
    5177                 :            : 
    5178                 :            :         /*
    5179                 :            :          * If a backend dies in an ugly way then we must signal all other backends
    5180                 :            :          * to quickdie.  If exit status is zero (normal) or one (FATAL exit), we
    5181                 :            :          * assume everything is all right and proceed to remove the backend from
    5182                 :            :          * the active backend list.
    5183                 :            :          */
    5184                 :            : #ifdef WIN32
    5185                 :            :         /*
    5186                 :            :          * On win32, also treat ERROR_WAIT_NO_CHILDREN (128) as nonfatal
    5187                 :            :          * case, since that sometimes happens under load when the process fails
    5188                 :            :          * to start properly (long before it starts using shared memory).
    5189                 :            :          */
    5190                 :            :         if (exitstatus == ERROR_WAIT_NO_CHILDREN)
    5191                 :            :         {
    5192                 :            :                 LogChildExit(LOG, _("server process"), pid, exitstatus);
    5193                 :            :                 exitstatus = 0;
    5194                 :            :         }
    5195                 :            : #endif
    5196                 :            : 
    5197 [ +  + ][ +  - ]:        837 :         if (!EXIT_STATUS_0(exitstatus) && !EXIT_STATUS_1(exitstatus))
                 [ -  + ]
    5198                 :            :         {
    5199                 :          0 :                 HandleChildCrash(pid, exitstatus, _("server process"));
    5200                 :          0 :                 return;
    5201                 :            :         }
    5202                 :            : 
    5203         [ +  - ]:       1017 :         for (curr = DLGetHead(BackendList); curr; curr = DLGetSucc(curr))
    5204                 :            :         {
    5205                 :       1017 :                 Backend    *bp = (Backend *) DLE_VAL(curr);
    5206                 :            : 
    5207         [ +  + ]:       1017 :                 if (bp->pid == pid)
    5208                 :            :                 {
    5209         [ +  + ]:        837 :                         if (!bp->dead_end)
    5210                 :            :                         {
    5211         [ -  + ]:        729 :                                 if (!ReleasePostmasterChildSlot(bp->child_slot))
    5212                 :            :                                 {
    5213                 :            :                                         /*
    5214                 :            :                                          * Uh-oh, the child failed to clean itself up.  Treat as a
    5215                 :            :                                          * crash after all.
    5216                 :            :                                          */
    5217                 :          0 :                                         HandleChildCrash(pid, exitstatus, _("server process"));
    5218                 :          0 :                                         return;
    5219                 :            :                                 }
    5220                 :            : #ifdef EXEC_BACKEND
    5221                 :            :                                 ShmemBackendArrayRemove(bp);
    5222                 :            : #endif
    5223                 :        729 :                         }
    5224                 :        837 :                         DLRemove(curr);
    5225                 :        837 :                         free(bp);
    5226                 :        837 :                         break;
    5227                 :            :                 }
    5228                 :        180 :         }
    5229                 :        837 : }
    5230                 :            : 
    5231                 :            : /*
    5232                 :            :  * HandleChildCrash -- cleanup after failed backend, bgwriter, walwriter,
    5233                 :            :  * or autovacuum.
    5234                 :            :  *
    5235                 :            :  * The objectives here are to clean up our local state about the child
    5236                 :            :  * process, and to signal all other remaining children to quickdie.
    5237                 :            :  *
    5238                 :            :  * @param pid the pid of the crashed child, or -1 if don't know the pid and don't want to log the failed child (this
    5239                 :            :  *            can happen when the filerep controller process signals us to reset, or when a primary/mirror peer
    5240                 :            :  *            signals us to reset)
    5241                 :            :  */
    5242                 :            : static void
    5243                 :            : HandleChildCrash(int pid, int exitstatus, const char *procname)
    5244                 :            : {
    5245                 :            :         Dlelem     *curr,
    5246                 :            :                            *next;
    5247                 :            :         Backend    *bp;
    5248                 :            : 
    5249                 :            :         /*
    5250                 :            :          * Make log entry unless there was a previous crash (if so, nonzero exit
    5251                 :            :          * status is to be expected in SIGQUIT response; don't clutter log)
    5252                 :            :          */
    5253         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!FatalError)
    5254                 :            :         {
    5255         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (pid != -1)
    5256                 :            :                 {
    5257                 :          0 :                         LogChildExit(LOG, procname, pid, exitstatus);
    5258                 :          0 :                 }
    5259         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
    5260                 :            :                                 (errmsg("terminating any other active server processes")));
    5261                 :          0 :         }
    5262                 :            : 
    5263                 :            :         /* Process regular backends */
    5264         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (curr = DLGetHead(BackendList); curr; curr = next)
    5265                 :            :         {
    5266                 :          0 :                 next = DLGetSucc(curr);
    5267                 :          0 :                 bp = (Backend *) DLE_VAL(curr);
    5268         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (bp->pid == pid)
    5269                 :            :                 {
    5270                 :            :                         /*
    5271                 :            :                          * Found entry for freshly-dead backend, so remove it.
    5272                 :            :                          */
    5273         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (!bp->dead_end)
    5274                 :            :                         {
    5275                 :          0 :                                 (void) ReleasePostmasterChildSlot(bp->child_slot);
    5276                 :            : #ifdef EXEC_BACKEND
    5277                 :            :                                 ShmemBackendArrayRemove(bp);
    5278                 :            : #endif
    5279                 :          0 :                         }
    5280                 :          0 :                         DLRemove(curr);
    5281                 :          0 :                         free(bp);
    5282                 :            :                         /* Keep looping so we can signal remaining backends */
    5283                 :          0 :                 }
    5284                 :            :                 else
    5285                 :            :                 {
    5286                 :            :                         /*
    5287                 :            :                          * This backend is still alive.  Unless we did so already, tell it
    5288                 :            :                          * to commit hara-kiri.
    5289                 :            :                          *
    5290                 :            :                          * SIGQUIT is the special signal that says exit without proc_exit
    5291                 :            :                          * and let the user know what's going on. But if SendStop is set
    5292                 :            :                          * (-s on command line), then we send SIGSTOP instead, so that we
    5293                 :            :                          * can get core dumps from all backends by hand.
    5294                 :            :                          *
    5295                 :            :                          * We could exclude dead_end children here, but at least in the
    5296                 :            :                          * SIGSTOP case it seems better to include them.
    5297                 :            :                          */
    5298         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (!FatalError)
    5299                 :            :                         {
    5300         [ #  # ]:          0 :                                 ereport((Debug_print_server_processes ? LOG : DEBUG2),
    5301                 :            :                                                 (errmsg_internal("sending %s to process %d",
    5302                 :            :                                                                                  (SendStop ? "SIGSTOP" : "SIGQUIT"),
    5303                 :            :                                                                                  (int) bp->pid)));
    5304                 :          0 :                                 signal_child(bp->pid, (SendStop ? SIGSTOP : SIGQUIT));
    5305                 :          0 :                         }
    5306                 :            :                 }
    5307                 :          0 :         }
    5308                 :            : 
    5309                 :            :         /* Take care of the startup process too */
    5310         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (pid == StartupPID)
    5311                 :          0 :                 StartupPID = 0;
    5312 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (StartupPID != 0 && !FatalError)
    5313                 :            :         {
    5314         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(DEBUG2,
    5315                 :            :                                 (errmsg_internal("sending %s to process %d",
    5316                 :            :                                                                  (SendStop ? "SIGSTOP" : "SIGQUIT"),
    5317                 :            :                                                                  (int) StartupPID)));
    5318                 :          0 :                 signal_child(StartupPID, (SendStop ? SIGSTOP : SIGQUIT));
    5319                 :          0 :         }
    5320                 :            : 
    5321                 :            :         /* Take care of the startup process too */
    5322         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (pid == StartupPass2PID)
    5323                 :          0 :                 StartupPass2PID = 0;
    5324 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (StartupPass2PID != 0 && !FatalError)
    5325                 :            :         {
    5326         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(DEBUG2,
    5327                 :            :                                 (errmsg_internal("sending %s to process %d",
    5328                 :            :                                                                  (SendStop ? "SIGSTOP" : "SIGQUIT"),
    5329                 :            :                                                                  (int) StartupPass2PID)));
    5330                 :          0 :                 signal_child(StartupPass2PID, (SendStop ? SIGSTOP : SIGQUIT));
    5331                 :          0 :         }
    5332                 :            : 
    5333                 :            :         /* Take care of the startup process too */
    5334         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (pid == StartupPass3PID)
    5335                 :          0 :                 StartupPass3PID = 0;
    5336 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (StartupPass3PID != 0 && !FatalError)
    5337                 :            :         {
    5338         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(DEBUG2,
    5339                 :            :                                 (errmsg_internal("sending %s to process %d",
    5340                 :            :                                                                  (SendStop ? "SIGSTOP" : "SIGQUIT"),
    5341                 :            :                                                                  (int) StartupPass3PID)));
    5342                 :          0 :                 signal_child(StartupPass3PID, (SendStop ? SIGSTOP : SIGQUIT));
    5343                 :          0 :         }

    5403                 :            :                          * own subprocesses, we want filerep to exit only when all its

    5524                 :            : // This is a duplicate of strsignal, for those platforms that have it.
    5525                 :            : static const char *
    5526                 :            : signal_to_name(int signal)
    5527                 :            : {
    5528                 :            : #if defined(HAVE_DECL_SYS_SIGLIST) && HAVE_DECL_SYS_SIGLIST
    5529                 :            :         // sys_siglist knows all possible signal names on ths platform.

    5531                 :          0 :                 return sys_siglist[signal];
    5532                 :            :         else
    5533                 :          0 :                 return NULL;
    5534                 :            : #else
    5535                 :            :         switch (signal)
    5536                 :            :         {
    5537                 :            :         case SIGINT:    return "SIGINT";
    5538                 :            :         case SIGTERM:   return "SIGTERM";
    5539                 :            :         case SIGQUIT:   return "SIGQUIT";
    5540                 :            :         case SIGSTOP:   return "SIGSTOP";
    5541                 :            :         case SIGUSR1:   return "SIGUSR1";
    5542                 :            :         case SIGUSR2:   return "SIGUSR2";
    5543                 :            :         default:                return NULL;
    5544                 :            :         }
    5545                 :            : #endif
    5546                 :          0 : }
