    5548                 :            : /*
    5549                 :            :  * Log the death of a child process.
    5550                 :            :  */
    5551                 :            : static void
    5552                 :            : LogChildExit(int lev, const char *procname, int pid, int exitstatus)
    5553                 :            : {
    5554         [ +  - ]:        837 :         if (WIFEXITED(exitstatus))
    5555         [ -  + ]:        837 :                 ereport(lev,
    5556                 :            : 
    5557                 :            :                 /*------
    5558                 :            :                   translator: %s is a noun phrase describing a child process, such as
    5559                 :            :                   "server process" */
    5560                 :            :                                 (errmsg("%s (PID %d) exited with exit code %d",
    5561                 :            :                                                 procname, pid, WEXITSTATUS(exitstatus))));
    5562 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (WIFSIGNALED(exitstatus))
    5563                 :            : #if defined(WIN32)
    5564                 :            :                 ereport(lev,
    5565                 :            : 
    5566                 :            :                 /*------
    5567                 :            :                   translator: %s is a noun phrase describing a child process, such as
    5568                 :            :                   "server process" */
    5569                 :            :                                 (errmsg("%s (PID %d) was terminated by exception 0x%X",
    5570                 :            :                                                 procname, pid, WTERMSIG(exitstatus)),
    5571                 :            :                                  errhint("See C include file \"ntstatus.h\" for a description of the hexadecimal value.")));
    5572                 :            : #elif defined(HAVE_DECL_SYS_SIGLIST) && HAVE_DECL_SYS_SIGLIST
    5573 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         ereport(lev,
    5574                 :            : 
    5575                 :            :         /*------
    5576                 :            :           translator: %s is a noun phrase describing a child process, such as
    5577                 :            :           "server process" */
    5578                 :            :                         (errmsg("%s (PID %d) was terminated by signal %d: %s",
    5579                 :            :                                         procname, pid, WTERMSIG(exitstatus),
    5580                 :            :                                         WTERMSIG(exitstatus) < NSIG ?
    5581                 :            :                                         sys_siglist[WTERMSIG(exitstatus)] : "(unknown)")));
    5582                 :            : #else
    5583                 :            :         {
    5584                 :            :                 // If we don't have strsignal or sys_siglist, do our own translation
    5585                 :            :                 const char *signalName;
    5586                 :            :                 signalName = signal_to_name(WTERMSIG(exitstatus));
    5587                 :            :                 if (signalName == NULL)
    5588                 :            :                         signalName = "(unknown)";
    5589                 :            :                 ereport(lev,
    5590                 :            : 
    5591                 :            :                 /*------
    5592                 :            :                   translator: %s is a noun phrase describing a child process, such as
    5593                 :            :                   "server process" */
    5594                 :            :                             (errmsg("%s (PID %d) was terminated by signal %d: %s",
    5595                 :            :                                                 procname, pid, WTERMSIG(exitstatus), signalName)));
    5596                 :            :         }
    5597                 :            : #endif
    5598                 :            :         else
    5599         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(lev,
    5600                 :            : 
    5601                 :            :                 /*------
    5602                 :            :                   translator: %s is a noun phrase describing a child process, such as
    5603                 :            :                   "server process" */
    5604                 :            :                                 (errmsg("%s (PID %d) exited with unrecognized status %d",
    5605                 :            :                                                 procname, pid, exitstatus)));
    5606                 :        837 : }

    5608                 :            : /**
    5609                 :            :  * POSTMASTER STATE MACHINE WAITING!
    5610                 :            :  */

    5612                 :            : /*
    5613                 :            :  *  Check to see if PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BACKENDS state is done
    5614                 :            :  */
    5615                 :            : static PMState
    5616                 :            : StateMachineCheck_WaitBackends(void)
    5617                 :            : {
    5618                 :          0 :     bool moveToNextState = false;
    5619                 :            : 
    5620 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :     Assert(pmState == PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BACKENDS);
    5621                 :            : 
    5622                 :            :     /*
    5623                 :            :      * If we are in a state-machine state that implies waiting for backends to
    5624                 :            :      * exit, see if they're all gone, and change state if so.
    5625                 :            :      */
    5626         [ #  # ]:          0 :     if (! isPrimaryMirrorModeAFullPostmaster(true))
    5627                 :            :     {
    5628                 :            :         /* wait_backends has ended because we don't have any! */
    5629                 :            : 
    5630                 :          0 :         moveToNextState = true;
    5631                 :          0 :     }
    5632                 :            :     else
    5633                 :            :     {
    5634                 :          0 :         int childCount = CountChildren(BACKEND_TYPE_AUTOVAC|BACKEND_TYPE_NORMAL);
    5635                 :          0 :         bool isFilerepBackendsDoneShutdown = IsFilerepBackendsDoneShutdown();
    5636                 :          0 :         bool autovacShutdown = AutoVacPID == 0;
    5637                 :            : 
    5638 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (childCount == 0 &&
    5639         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         WalReceiverPID == 0 &&
    5640         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         WalWriterPID == 0 &&
    5641 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :             (BgWriterPID == 0 || !FatalError) && /* todo: CHAD_PM why wait for BgWriterPID here?  Can't we just allow
    5642                 :            :                                                           normal state advancement to hit there? */
    5643         [ #  # ]:          0 :             autovacShutdown &&
    5644                 :          0 :             isFilerepBackendsDoneShutdown)
    5645                 :            :         {
    5646                 :            :             /*
    5647                 :            :                          * Note: on fatal error, children are killed all at once by
    5648                 :            :                          * HandleChildCrash, so asserts are not valid
    5649                 :            :                          */
    5650         [ #  # ]:          0 :             if (!FatalError)
    5651                 :            :             {
    5652 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(StartupPID == 0);
    5653                 :          0 :             }
    5654                 :            : 
    5655                 :            :             /*
    5656                 :            :              * PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BACKENDS state ends when we have no regular backends
    5657                 :            :              * (including autovac workers) and no walwriter or autovac launcher.
    5658                 :            :              * If we are doing crash recovery then we expect the bgwriter to exit
    5659                 :            :              * too, otherwise not.      The archiver, stats, and syslogger processes
    5660                 :            :              * are disregarded since they are not connected to shared memory; we
    5661                 :            :              * also disregard dead_end children here.
    5662                 :            :              */
    5663         [ #  # ]:          0 :             if (FatalError)
    5664                 :            :             {
    5665                 :          0 :                 moveToNextState = true;
    5666                 :          0 :             }
    5667                 :            :             else
    5668                 :            :             {
    5669 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(Shutdown > NoShutdown);
    5670                 :          0 :                 moveToNextState = true;
    5671                 :            :             }
    5672                 :          0 :         }
    5673                 :            :         else
    5674                 :            :         {
    5675         [ #  # ]:          0 :             if (Debug_print_server_processes)
    5676                 :            :             {
    5677                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "Postmaster State Machine: waiting on backends, children %d, filerep backends %s, av %s",
    5678                 :            :                     childCount,
    5679                 :            :                     isFilerepBackendsDoneShutdown ? "stopped" : "running",
    5680                 :            :                     autovacShutdown ? "stopped" : "running" );
    5681                 :          0 :             }
    5682                 :            :         }
    5683                 :            :     }
    5684                 :            : 
    5685         [ #  # ]:          0 :     return moveToNextState ? (pmState+1) : pmState;
    5686                 :            : }

    5688                 :            : /**

    5701                 :            :                 case PEER_RESET_FAILED:
    5702                 :            : 
    5703                 :            :                         /* mark fault in shared memory */
    5704                 :          0 :                         setTransitionToFault();
    5705                 :            : 
    5706                 :          0 :                         RestartAfterPostmasterReset();

    5715                 :            : /**
    5716                 :            :  * Check to see if PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_DEAD_END_CHILDREN state is done

    5718                 :            : static PMState StateMachineCheck_WaitDeadEndChildren(void)
    5719                 :            : {
    5720                 :          0 :     bool moveToNextState = false;
    5721 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :     Assert(pmState == PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_DEAD_END_CHILDREN );
    5722                 :            : 
    5723                 :            :     /*
    5724                 :            :      * PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_DEAD_END_CHILDREN state ends when the BackendList is entirely empty
    5725                 :            :      * (ie, no dead_end children remain), and the archiver and stats
    5726                 :            :      * collector are gone too.
    5727                 :            :      *
    5728                 :            :      * The reason we wait for those two is to protect them against a new
    5729                 :            :      * postmaster starting conflicting subprocesses; this isn't an
    5730                 :            :      * ironclad protection, but it at least helps in the
    5731                 :            :      * shutdown-and-immediately-restart scenario.  Note that they have
    5732                 :            :      * already been sent appropriate shutdown signals, either during a
    5733                 :            :      * normal state transition leading up to PM_WAIT_DEAD_END, or during
    5734                 :            :      * FatalError processing.

    5737                 :            :     {
    5738                 :            :         /* These other guys should be dead already */
    5739                 :            :         /* todo: ALL other processes should be dead at this point */
    5740                 :            :         /* note: on fatal error, children are killed all at once by HandleChildCrash, so asserts are not valid */
    5741         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if ( ! FatalError )
    5742                 :            :         {
    5743 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :             Assert(StartupPID == 0);
    5744 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :             Assert(BgWriterPID == 0);
    5745 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :             Assert(CheckpointerPID == 0);
    5746 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :             Assert(AutoVacPID == 0);
    5747                 :          0 :         }
    5748                 :            :         /* syslogger is not considered here */
    5749                 :          0 :         moveToNextState = true;
    5750                 :            : 
    5751                 :          0 :     }
    5752                 :            : 
    5753         [ #  # ]:          0 :     return moveToNextState ? (pmState+1) : pmState;
    5754                 :            : }

    5760                 :            : {

    5762                 :            : 
    5763 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :     bool moveToNextState = StartupPID == 0 && StartupPass2PID == 0 && StartupPass3PID == 0 && StartupPass4PID == 0;

    5766                 :            : 
    5767                 :            : /**
    5768                 :            :  * Check to see if PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_PREBGWRITER state is done
    5769                 :            :  */
    5770                 :            : static PMState StateMachineCheck_WaitPreBgWriter(void)
    5771                 :            : {
    5772 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :     Assert(pmState == PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_PREBGWRITER );
    5773                 :            : 
    5774                 :            :     /* waiting for pre-bgwriter services to exit */
    5775                 :          0 :     bool moveToNextState = 
    5776         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 (!ServiceProcessesExist(/* excludeFlags */ PMSUBPROC_FLAG_STOP_AFTER_BGWRITER) &&
    5777                 :          0 :                  CheckpointerPID == 0);
    5778         [ #  # ]:          0 :     return moveToNextState ? (pmState+1) : pmState;
    5779                 :            : }
    5780                 :            : 
    5781                 :            : /**
    5782                 :            :  * Check to see if PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_POSTBGWRITER state is done
    5783                 :            :  */
    5784                 :            : static PMState StateMachineCheck_WaitPostBgWriter(void)
    5785                 :            : {
    5786 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :     Assert(pmState == PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_POSTBGWRITER);
    5787                 :            :

    5789                 :          0 :     bool moveToNextState = true;
    5790                 :            : 
    5791                 :            :     /* note: on fatal error, children are killed all at once by HandleChildCrash, so asserts are not valid */
    5792         [ #  # ]:          0 :     if ( ! FatalError )
    5793                 :            :     {
    5794 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(BgWriterPID == 0);
    5795                 :          0 :     }
    5796                 :            : 
    5797         [ #  # ]:          0 :     if ( ServiceProcessesExist(/* excludeFlags */ 0))
    5798                 :          0 :         moveToNextState = false;
    5799 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :     else if ( FilerepPID  != 0 ||
    5800         [ #  # ]:          0 :         PgArchPID  != 0 ||
    5801         [ #  # ]:          0 :         PgStatPID  != 0 ||
    5802                 :          0 :         FilerepPeerResetPID != 0)
    5803                 :            :     {
    5804                 :          0 :         moveToNextState = false;
    5805                 :          0 :     }
    5806                 :            :         /*
    5807                 :            :          * If there are any backends still alive we can't move to next state
    5808                 :            :          * *Note* here that backends here mean simply normal, autovac workers and
    5809                 :            :          * WAL Sender (No dead_end backends)
    5810                 :            :          */
    5811         [ #  # ]:          0 :         else if (CountChildren(BACKEND_TYPE_ALL) != 0)
    5812                 :          0 :                 moveToNextState = false;
    5813                 :            : 
    5814         [ #  # ]:          0 :     return moveToNextState ? (pmState+1) : pmState;
    5815                 :            : }
    5816                 :            : 
    5817                 :            : /**
    5818                 :            :  * Check to see if PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BGWRITER_CHECKPOINT state is done

    5823                 :            : 
    5824                 :            :     /* waiting for bgwriter to FINISH ckpt and exit */
    5825                 :          0 :     bool moveToNextState = BgWriterPID == 0;

    5830                 :            : /***************************************
    5831                 :            :  * POSTMASTER STATE MACHINE TRANSITIONS
    5832                 :            :  ***************************************/
    5833                 :            : 
    5834                 :            : /**
    5835                 :            :  * Called to transition to PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_STARTUP_PROCESSES -- so tell any startup processes to complete
    5836                 :            :  */
    5837                 :            : static void
    5838                 :            : StateMachineTransition_ShutdownStartupProcesses(void)
    5839                 :            : {
    5840                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(StartupPID, SIGTERM);
    5841                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(StartupPass2PID, SIGTERM);
    5842                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(StartupPass3PID, SIGTERM);
    5843                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(StartupPass4PID, SIGTERM);
    5844                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(WalReceiverPID, SIGTERM);
    5845                 :          0 : }
    5846                 :            : 
    5847                 :            : /*
    5848                 :            :  * Called to transition to PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BACKENDS : waiting for all backends to finish
    5849                 :            :  */
    5850                 :            : static void
    5851                 :            : StateMachineTransition_ShutdownBackends(void)
    5852                 :            : {
    5853         [ #  # ]:          0 :     if (Shutdown == FastShutdown)
    5854                 :            :     {
    5855                 :            :         /* shut down all backends, including autovac workers */
    5856                 :          0 :         SignalChildren(SIGTERM);
    5857                 :          0 :     }
    5858                 :            :     else
    5859                 :            :     {
    5860                 :            :         /* only autovacuum workers are told to shut down immediately */
    5861                 :          0 :         SignalAutovacWorkers(SIGTERM);
    5862                 :            :     }
    5863                 :            : 
    5864                 :            :     /* and the autovac launcher too */
    5865                 :          0 :     signal_child_if_up(AutoVacPID, SIGTERM);
    5866                 :            : 
    5867                 :            :         /* and the wal writer too */
    5868                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(WalWriterPID, SIGTERM);
    5869                 :            : 
    5870                 :          0 :     signal_filerep_to_shutdown(SegmentStateShutdownFilerepBackends);
    5871                 :          0 : }
    5872                 :            : 
    5873                 :            : /**
    5874                 :            :  * Called to transition to PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_PREBGWRITER : waiting for pre-bgwriter processes to finish
    5875                 :            :  */
    5876                 :            : static void
    5877                 :            : StateMachineTransition_ShutdownPreBgWriter(void)
    5878                 :            : {
    5879                 :            :     /* SIGUSR2, regardless of shutdown mode */
    5880                 :          0 :     StopServices(/* excludeFlags */ PMSUBPROC_FLAG_STOP_AFTER_BGWRITER, SIGUSR2 );
    5881                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(CheckpointerPID, SIGUSR2);
    5882                 :          0 : }
    5883                 :            : 
    5884                 :            : /**
    5885                 :            :  * Called to transition to PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BGWRITER_CHECKPOINT : waiting for bgwriter to run a checkpoint and quit
    5886                 :            :  */
    5887                 :            : static void
    5888                 :            : StateMachineTransition_ShutdownBgWriterWithCheckpoint(void)
    5889                 :            : {
    5890                 :            :         /*
    5891                 :            :          * In case of some fatal error including where we are not ready to issue
    5892                 :            :          * checkpoint, we don't want to start bgwriter.  Also, if we were in the
    5893                 :            :          * recovery process (i.e. standby mode), we don't want it to run.
    5894                 :            :          */
    5895 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!FatalError &&
    5896         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 !RecoveryError &&
    5897         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 isFullPostmasterAndDatabaseIsAllowed() &&
    5898                 :          0 :                 !RecoveryInProgress())
    5899                 :            :         {
    5900                 :            :                 /* Start the bgwriter if not running */
    5901         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (BgWriterPID == 0 )
    5902                 :            :                 {
    5903                 :          0 :                         SetBGWriterPID(StartBackgroundWriter());
    5904                 :          0 :                 }
    5905                 :          0 :         }
    5906                 :            : 
    5907                 :            :         /* SIGUSR2, regardless of shutdown mode */
    5908                 :          0 :         signal_child_if_up(BgWriterPID, SIGUSR2);
    5909                 :          0 : }
    5910                 :            : 
    5911                 :            : /**
    5912                 :            :  * Called to transition to PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_POSTBGWRITER  : waiting for post-bgwriter services to exit
    5913                 :            :  */
    5914                 :            : static void StateMachineTransition_ShutdownPostBgWriter(void)
    5915                 :            : {
    5916                 :            :     /* these services take the same signal regardless of fast vs smart shutdown */
    5917                 :          0 :     StopServices(/* excludeFlags */ 0, SIGUSR2);
    5918                 :          0 :     signal_filerep_to_shutdown(SegmentStateShutdown);
    5919                 :          0 :     signal_child_if_up(PgArchPID, SIGQUIT);
    5920                 :          0 :     signal_child_if_up(PgStatPID, SIGQUIT);
    5921                 :            : 
    5922                 :            :     /* this process is probably not running,
    5923                 :            :      * but the shutdown sequence may have been started in the middle of our reset */
    5924                 :          0 :     signal_child_if_up(FilerepPeerResetPID, SIGQUIT);
    5925                 :            : 
    5926                 :            :         /*
    5927                 :            :          * Waken walsenders for the last time. (Ideally)No regular backends
    5928                 :            :          * should be around anymore.
    5929                 :            :          */
    5930 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(CountChildren(BACKEND_TYPE_NORMAL|BACKEND_TYPE_AUTOVAC) == 0);
    5931                 :          0 :         SignalChildren(SIGUSR2);
    5932                 :          0 : }
    5933                 :            : 
    5934                 :            : /**
    5935                 :            :  * Called to transition tp PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_DEAD_END_CHILDREN : waiting for dead-end children to complete
    5936                 :            :  */
    5937                 :            : static void StateMachineTransition_ShutdownDeadEndChildren(void)
    5938                 :            : {
    5939                 :            :     /* nothing to do, just waiting */
    5940                 :          0 : }
    5941                 :            : 
    5942                 :            : /**
    5943                 :            :  * Called to transition to final PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_NO_CHILDREN state.  Note that in some cases this will
    5944                 :            :  *   actually exit the process because a requested shutdown is complete!
    5945                 :            :  */
    5946                 :            : static void StateMachineTransition_NoChildren(void)
    5947                 :            : {
    5948 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :      Assert(pmState == PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_NO_CHILDREN);
    5949         [ #  # ]:          0 :      if(Shutdown > NoShutdown)
    5950                 :            :      {
    5951                 :            :          /*
    5952                 :            :           * If we've been told to shut down, we exit as soon as there are no
    5953                 :            :           * remaining children. If there was a crash, cleanup will occur at the
    5954                 :            :           * next startup.  (Before PostgreSQL 8.3, we tried to recover from the
    5955                 :            :           * crash before exiting, but that seems unwise if we are quitting because
    5956                 :            :           * we got SIGTERM from init --- there may well not be time for recovery
    5957                 :            :           * before init decides to SIGKILL us.)
    5958                 :            :           *
    5959                 :            :           * Note that the syslogger continues to run.  It will exit when it sees
    5960                 :            :           * EOF on its input pipe, which happens when there are no more upstream
    5961                 :            :           * processes.
    5962                 :            :           */
    5963         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (FatalError)
    5964                 :            :         {
    5965         [ #  # ]:          0 :             ereport(LOG, (errmsg("abnormal database system shutdown"),errSendAlert(true)));
    5966                 :          0 :             ExitPostmaster(1);
    5967                 :          0 :         }
    5968                 :            :         else
    5969                 :            :         {
    5970                 :            :             /*
    5971                 :            :              * Terminate backup mode to avoid recovery after a clean fast
    5972                 :            :              * shutdown.
    5973                 :            :              */
    5974                 :            :              /* GPDB doesn't have backup mode, so nothing to cancel */
    5975                 :            :             //CancelBackup();
    5976                 :            : 
    5977                 :            :             /* Normal exit from the postmaster is here */
    5978                 :          0 :             ExitPostmaster(0);
    5979                 :            :         }
    5980                 :          0 :     }
    5981         [ #  # ]:          0 :     else if (RecoveryError)
    5982                 :            :     {
    5983         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ereport(LOG,
    5984                 :            :             (errmsg("database recovery failed; exiting")));
    5985                 :            : 
    5986                 :            :         /*
    5987                 :            :          * If recovery failed, wait for all non-syslogger children to exit, and
    5988                 :            :          * then exit postmaster. We don't try to reinitialize when recovery fails,
    5989                 :            :          * because more than likely it will just fail again and we will keep
    5990                 :            :          * trying forever.
    5991                 :            :          */
    5992                 :          0 :         ExitPostmaster(1);
    5993                 :          0 :     }
    5994         [ #  # ]:          0 :     else if (FatalError)
    5995                 :            :     {
    5996                 :          0 :         BeginResetOfPostmasterAfterChildrenAreShutDown();
    5997                 :          0 :     }
    5998                 :            :     else
    5999                 :            :     {
    6000         [ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(!"NoChildren state reached when not in shutdown and not experiencing a fatal error");
    6001                 :            :     }
    6002                 :          0 : }
    6003                 :            : 
    6004                 :            : 
    6005                 :            :  /*
    6006                 :            :  * Advance the postmaster's state machine and take actions as appropriate
    6007                 :            :  *
    6008                 :            :  *
    6009                 :            :  * Right now, this state machine is concerned with the orderly shutdown sequence.
    6010                 :            :  * We should be able to make it cover startup and PM_RUN as well (where we restart crashed processes)
    6011                 :            :  *
    6012                 :            :  * Note also that because of the fact that it only handles orderly shutdown directly, pmState is
    6013                 :            :  *   sometimes changed by a transition or wait function (as when resetting the filerep peer during postmaster
    6014                 :            :  *   reset).  This could probably be structured better so it all worked better
    6015                 :            :  *
    6016                 :            :  *
    6017                 :            :  * This is common code for pmdie() and reaper(), which receive the signals
    6018                 :            :  * that might mean we need to change state.
    6019                 :            :  *
    6020                 :            :  */
    6021                 :            : static void
    6022                 :            : PostmasterStateMachine(void)
    6023                 :            : {
    6024                 :            :     int passThroughStateMachine;
    6025                 :        945 :     const int minStateForLogging = PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN;
    6026                 :            : 
    6027                 :            : #define DO_STATE_MACHINE_LOGGING (pmState >= minStateForLogging && Debug_print_server_processes)
    6028                 :            : 
    6029                 :        945 :     for (passThroughStateMachine = 0;; passThroughStateMachine++)
    6030                 :            :     {
    6031                 :        945 :         int nextPmState = pmState;
    6032 [ +  - ][ -  + ]:        945 :         Assert(passThroughStateMachine < 100);
    6033                 :            : 
    6034 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:        945 :         if (DO_STATE_MACHINE_LOGGING)
    6035                 :            :         {
    6036                 :          0 :             elog(LOG, "Postmaster State Machine: checking state %s", gPmStateLabels[pmState]);
    6037                 :          0 :         }
    6038                 :            : 
    6039                 :            :         /* first, look at the current pmState and see if that state has been completed */
    6040   [ -  -  -  -  :        945 :         switch ( pmState )
          -  -  -  +  -  
          -  -  -  -  -  
             -  -  -  - ]
    6041                 :            :         {
    6042                 :            :             case PM_INIT:
    6043                 :            :             case PM_STARTUP:
    6044                 :            :             case PM_STARTUP_PASS2:
    6045                 :            :                         case PM_STARTUP_PASS3:
    6046                 :            :                         case PM_STARTUP_PASS4:
    6047                 :            :             case PM_RECOVERY:
    6048                 :            :             case PM_RECOVERY_CONSISTENT:
    6049                 :            :             case PM_RUN:
    6050                 :        945 :                 nextPmState = pmState;
    6051                 :        945 :                 break;
    6052                 :            : 
    6053                 :            :             /* immediately move to next step and run its transition actions ... */
    6054                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN:
    6055                 :          0 :                 nextPmState = pmState + 1;
    6056                 :          0 :                 break;
    6057                 :            : 
    6058                 :            :             /* shutdown has been requested -- check to make sure startup processes are done */
    6059                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_STARTUP_PROCESSES:
    6060                 :          0 :                 nextPmState = StateMachineCheck_WaitStartupProcesses();
    6061                 :          0 :                 break;
    6062                 :            : 
    6063                 :            :             /* waiting for live backends to exit */
    6064                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BACKENDS:
    6065                 :          0 :                 nextPmState = StateMachineCheck_WaitBackends();
    6066                 :          0 :                 break;
    6067                 :            : 
    6068                 :            :             /* waiting for pre-backend services to exit */
    6069                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_PREBGWRITER:
    6070                 :          0 :                 nextPmState = StateMachineCheck_WaitPreBgWriter();
    6071                 :          0 :                 break;
    6072                 :            : 
    6073                 :            :             /* waiting for bgwriter to do shutdown ckpt */
    6074                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BGWRITER_CHECKPOINT:
    6075                 :          0 :                 nextPmState = StateMachineCheck_WaitBgWriterCheckpointComplete();
    6076                 :          0 :                 break;
    6077                 :            : 
    6078                 :            :             /* waiting for pre-backend services to exit */
    6079                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_POSTBGWRITER:
    6080                 :          0 :                 nextPmState = StateMachineCheck_WaitPostBgWriter();
    6081                 :          0 :                 break;
    6082                 :            : 
    6083                 :            :             /* waiting for dead_end children to exit */
    6084                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_DEAD_END_CHILDREN:
    6085                 :          0 :                 nextPmState = StateMachineCheck_WaitDeadEndChildren();
    6086                 :          0 :                 break;
    6087                 :            : 
    6088                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_NO_CHILDREN:
    6089         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(!"Unreachable state");
    6090                 :          0 :                 break;
    6091                 :            : 
    6092                 :            :                 case PM_POSTMASTER_RESET_FILEREP_PEER:
    6093                 :          0 :                         nextPmState = StateMachineCheck_WaitFilerepPeerReset();
    6094                 :          0 :                 break;
    6095                 :            : 
    6096                 :            :             default:
    6097         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(!"Invalid pmState");
    6098                 :          0 :                 break;
    6099                 :            :         }
    6100                 :            : 
    6101         [ -  + ]:        945 :         if ( nextPmState != pmState )
    6102                 :            :         {
    6103                 :          0 :             pmState = nextPmState;
    6104                 :            : 
    6105 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :             if ( DO_STATE_MACHINE_LOGGING )
    6106                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "Postmaster State Machine: entering state %s", gPmStateLabels[pmState]);
    6107                 :          0 :         }
    6108         [ -  + ]:        945 :         else if ( pmStateMachineMustRunActions)
    6109                 :            :         {
    6110 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :             if ( DO_STATE_MACHINE_LOGGING )
    6111                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "Postmaster State Machine: rerunning actions for %s", gPmStateLabels[pmState]);
    6112                 :          0 :         }
    6113                 :            :         else
    6114                 :            :         {
    6115 [ -  + ][ #  # ]:        945 :             if ( DO_STATE_MACHINE_LOGGING )
    6116                 :          0 :                 elog(LOG, "Postmaster State Machine: no change from %s", gPmStateLabels[pmState]);
    6117                 :            :                 
    6118                 :            :             /* current state was not completed ... quit and wait to be called again */
    6119                 :        945 :             break;
    6120                 :            :         }
    6121                 :            : 
    6122                 :          0 :         pmStateMachineMustRunActions = false;
    6123                 :            : 
    6124   [ #  #  #  #  :          0 :         switch ( pmState )
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    6125                 :            :         {
    6126                 :            :             case PM_INIT:
    6127                 :            :             case PM_STARTUP:
    6128                 :            :             case PM_STARTUP_PASS2:
    6129                 :            :                         case PM_STARTUP_PASS3:
    6130                 :            :                         case PM_STARTUP_PASS4:
    6131                 :            :             case PM_RECOVERY:
    6132                 :            :             case PM_RECOVERY_CONSISTENT:
    6133                 :            :             case PM_RUN:
    6134                 :          0 :                 break;
    6135                 :            : 
    6136                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN:
    6137 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert("Unreachable state; the only change of pmState to PM_CHILD_STOP_BEGIN is in the pmdie handler.");
    6138                 :          0 :                 break;
    6139                 :            : 
    6140                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_STARTUP_PROCESSES:
    6141                 :            :                 /* shutdown has been requested -- check to make sure startup processes are done */
    6142                 :          0 :                 StateMachineTransition_ShutdownStartupProcesses();
    6143                 :          0 :                 break;
    6144                 :            : 
    6145                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BACKENDS:
    6146                 :            :                 /* tell live backends to exit */
    6147                 :          0 :                 StateMachineTransition_ShutdownBackends();
    6148                 :          0 :                 break;
    6149                 :            : 
    6150                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_PREBGWRITER :
    6151                 :            :                 /* tell pre-bgwriter services to exit */
    6152                 :          0 :                 StateMachineTransition_ShutdownPreBgWriter();
    6153                 :          0 :                 break;
    6154                 :            : 
    6155                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_BGWRITER_CHECKPOINT:
    6156                 :            :                 /* tell bgwriter to do shutdown ckpt and exit */
    6157                 :          0 :                 StateMachineTransition_ShutdownBgWriterWithCheckpoint();
    6158                 :          0 :                 break;
    6159                 :            : 
    6160                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_POSTBGWRITER :
    6161                 :            :                 /* tell post-bgwriter services to exit */
    6162                 :          0 :                 StateMachineTransition_ShutdownPostBgWriter();
    6163                 :          0 :                 break;
    6164                 :            : 
    6165                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_DEAD_END_CHILDREN:
    6166                 :            :                 /* waiting for dead_end children to exit */
    6167                 :          0 :                 StateMachineTransition_ShutdownDeadEndChildren();
    6168                 :          0 :                 break;
    6169                 :            : 
    6170                 :            :             case PM_CHILD_STOP_WAIT_NO_CHILDREN:
    6171                 :            :                 /* all children are done, now we can exit or restart after crash, perhaps set up a transition to
    6172                 :            :                  *  StateMachineTransition_ResetFilerepPeer
    6173                 :            :                  */
    6174                 :          0 :                 StateMachineTransition_NoChildren();
    6175                 :          0 :                 break;
    6176                 :            : 
    6177                 :            :                 case PM_POSTMASTER_RESET_FILEREP_PEER:
    6178         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         Assert(!"Invalid pmState PM_POSTMASTER_RESET_FILEREP_PEER : "
    6179                 :            :                                         "is not reached through a nextPmState assignment");
    6180                 :          0 :                         break;
    6181                 :            : 
    6182                 :            :             default:
    6183         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 Assert(!"Invalid pmState");
    6184                 :          0 :                 break;
    6185                 :            :         }
    6186                 :            : 
    6187                 :            :         /* and now loop again to see if the new pmState has already been satisfied, most likely if our
    6188                 :            :          *   current actions on entering the state were no-ops */
    6189                 :          0 :     }
    6190                 :        945 : }
    6191                 :            : 
    6192                 :            : 
    6193                 :            : /*
    6194                 :            :  * Send a signal to a postmaster child process
    6195                 :            :  *
    6196                 :            :  * On systems that have setsid(), each child process sets itself up as a
    6197                 :            :  * process group leader.  For signals that are generally interpreted in the
    6198                 :            :  * appropriate fashion, we signal the entire process group not just the
    6199                 :            :  * direct child process.  This allows us to, for example, SIGQUIT a blocked
    6200                 :            :  * archive_recovery script, or SIGINT a script being run by a backend via
    6201                 :            :  * system().
    6202                 :            :  *
    6203                 :            :  * There is a race condition for recently-forked children: they might not
    6204                 :            :  * have executed setsid() yet.  So we signal the child directly as well as
    6205                 :            :  * the group.  We assume such a child will handle the signal before trying

    6207                 :            :  * child twice will not cause any problems.

    6212                 :            : 
    6213         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (Debug_print_server_processes)
    6214                 :            :         {
    6215                 :            :                 const char *procName;
    6216                 :            :                 const char *signalName;
    6217                 :            : 
    6218                 :          0 :                 procName = GetServerProcessTitle(pid);
    6219                 :          0 :                 signalName = signal_to_name(signal);
    6220                 :            : 
    6221         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (signalName != NULL)
    6222         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         elog(LOG,"signal %s sent to '%s' pid %ld",
    6223                 :            :                              signalName, (procName ? procName : "unknown"), (long)pid);
    6224                 :            :                 else
    6225         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         elog(LOG,"signal %d sent to '%s' pid %ld",
    6226                 :            :                              signal, (procName ? procName : "unknown"), (long)pid);
    6227                 :          0 :         }
    6228                 :            : 
    6229         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (kill(pid, signal) < 0)

    6232   [ -  -  -  -  :         54 :         switch (signal)
                      + ]
    6233                 :            :         {
    6234                 :            :                 case SIGINT:
    6235                 :            :                 case SIGTERM:
    6236                 :            :                 case SIGQUIT:
    6237                 :            :                 case SIGSTOP:

    6247                 :            : /*
    6248                 :            :  * Send a signal to the targeted children (but NOT special children;
    6249                 :            :  * dead_end children are never signaled, either).
    6250                 :            :  */

    6252                 :            : SignalSomeChildren(int signal, int target)
    6253                 :            : {
    6254                 :            :         Dlelem     *curr;
    6255                 :          0 :         bool            signaled = false;
    6256                 :            : 
    6257         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (curr = DLGetHead(BackendList); curr; curr = DLGetSucc(curr))
    6258                 :            :         {
    6259                 :          0 :                 Backend    *bp = (Backend *) DLE_VAL(curr);
    6260                 :            : 
    6261         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (bp->dead_end)
    6262                 :          0 :                         continue;
    6263                 :            : 
    6264                 :            :                 /*
    6265                 :            :                  * Since target == BACKEND_TYPE_ALL is the most common case, we test
    6266                 :            :                  * it first and avoid touching shared memory for every child.
    6267                 :            :                  */
    6268         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (target != BACKEND_TYPE_ALL)
    6269                 :            :                 {
    6270                 :            :                         int                     child;
    6271                 :            : 
    6272         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (bp->is_autovacuum)
    6273                 :          0 :                                 child = BACKEND_TYPE_AUTOVAC;
    6274         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         else if (IsPostmasterChildWalSender(bp->child_slot))
    6275                 :          0 :                                 child = BACKEND_TYPE_WALSND;
    6276                 :            :                         else
    6277                 :          0 :                                 child = BACKEND_TYPE_NORMAL;
    6278         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         if (!(target & child))
    6279                 :          0 :                                 continue;
    6280                 :          0 :                 }
    6281                 :            : 
    6282         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(DEBUG4,
    6283                 :            :                                 (errmsg_internal("sending signal %d to process %d",
    6284                 :            :                                                                  signal, (int) bp->pid)));
    6285                 :          0 :                 signal_child(bp->pid, signal);
    6286                 :          0 :                 signaled = true;
    6287                 :          0 :         }
    6288                 :          0 :         return signaled;
    6289                 :            : }

    6291                 :            : /*

    6312                 :            :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OUT_OF_MEMORY),
    6313                 :            :                                  errmsg("out of memory")));

    6315                 :            :         }
    6316                 :            :

    6324                 :            :

    6327                 :        891 :         bn->dead_end = (port->canAcceptConnections != CAC_OK);
    6328                 :            :

    6330                 :            :          * Unless it's a dead_end child, assign it a child slot number

    6336                 :            : 
    6337                 :            : #ifdef EXEC_BACKEND

    6352                 :            : 
    6353                 :         54 :                 MyProcPid = getpid();   /* reset MyProcPid */
    6354                 :            : 
    6355                 :         54 :                 MyStartTime = time(NULL);
    6356                 :            :

    6407                 :            : /*
    6408                 :            :  * Try to report backend fork() failure to client before we close the
    6409                 :            :  * connection.  Since we do not care to risk blocking the postmaster on
    6410                 :            :  * this connection, we set the connection to non-blocking and try only once.
    6411                 :            :  *

    6413                 :            :  * it's not up and running.
    6414                 :            :  */
    6415                 :            : static void
    6416                 :            : report_fork_failure_to_client(Port *port, int errnum)
    6417                 :            : {
    6418                 :            :         char            buffer[1000];
    6419                 :            :         int                     rc;
    6420                 :            : 
    6421                 :            :         /* Format the error message packet (always V2 protocol) */
    6422                 :          0 :         snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "E%s%s\n",
    6423                 :            :                          _("could not fork new process for connection: "),
    6424                 :            :                          strerror(errnum));
    6425                 :            : 
    6426                 :            :         /* Set port to non-blocking.  Don't do send() if this fails */
    6427         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (!pg_set_noblock(port->sock))
    6428                 :          0 :                 return;
    6429                 :            : 
    6430                 :            :         /* We'll retry after EINTR, but ignore all other failures */
    6431                 :          0 :         do
    6432                 :            :         {
    6433                 :          0 :                 rc = send(port->sock, buffer, strlen(buffer) + 1, 0);
    6434 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         } while (rc < 0 && errno == EINTR);
    6435                 :          0 : }

    6437                 :            : 
    6438                 :            : /*
    6439                 :            :  * BackendInitialize -- initialize an interactive (postmaster-child)
    6440                 :            :  *                              backend process, and perform client authentication.
    6441                 :            :  *
    6442                 :            :  * returns: nothing.  Will not return at all if there's any failure.
    6443                 :            :  *

    6447                 :            :  */

    6449                 :            : BackendInitialize(Port *port)

    6452                 :            :         char            remote_host[NI_MAXHOST];

    6459                 :            :         /*

    6461                 :            :          * authentication cycle: it can be set in postgresql.conf to allow time to
    6462                 :            :          * attach to the newly-forked backend with a debugger. (See also the -W
    6463                 :            :          * backend switch, which we allow clients to pass through PGOPTIONS, but
    6464                 :            :          * it is not honored until after authentication.)
    6465                 :            :          */
    6466         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (PreAuthDelay > 0)
    6467                 :          0 :                 pg_usleep(PreAuthDelay * 1000000L);
    6468                 :            : 
    6469                 :            :         /* This flag will remain set until InitPostgres finishes authentication */
    6470                 :         54 :         ClientAuthInProgress = true;    /* limit visibility of log messages */
    6471                 :            : 
    6472                 :            :         /* save process start time */

    6474                 :         54 :         MyStartTime = timestamptz_to_time_t(port->SessionStartTime);
    6475                 :            : 
    6476                 :            :         /* set these to empty in case they are needed before we set them up */
    6477                 :         54 :         port->remote_host = "";
    6478                 :         54 :         port->remote_port = "";
    6479                 :            :

    6481                 :            :          * Initialize libpq and enable reporting of ereport errors to the client.

    6483                 :            :          */

    6486                 :            :

    6492                 :            : #ifdef HAVE_SETSID
    6493         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (setsid() < 0)

    6495                 :            : #endif
    6496                 :            : 
    6497                 :            :         /*
    6498                 :            :          * We arrange for a simple exit(1) if we receive SIGTERM or SIGQUIT during
    6499                 :            :          * any client authentication related communication. Otherwise the
    6500                 :            :          * postmaster cannot shutdown the database FAST or IMMED cleanly if a
    6501                 :            :          * buggy client blocks a backend during authentication. XXX it follows that
    6502                 :            :          * the remainder of this function must tolerate losing control at any
    6503                 :            :          * instant.  Likewise, any pg_on_exit_callback registered before or during
    6504                 :            :          * this function must be prepared to execute at any instant between here
    6505                 :            :          * and the end of this function.  Furthermore, affected callbacks execute
    6506                 :            :          * partially or not at all when a second exit-inducing signal arrives
    6507                 :            :          * after proc_exit_prepare() decrements on_proc_exit_index.  (Thanks to
    6508                 :            :          * that mechanic, callbacks need not anticipate more than one call.)  This
    6509                 :            :          * is fragile; it ought to instead follow the norm of handling interrupts

    6513                 :         54 :         pqsignal(SIGQUIT, authdie);
    6514                 :         54 :         pqsignal(SIGALRM, authdie);
    6515                 :         54 :         PG_SETMASK(&StartupBlockSig);
    6516                 :            : 
    6517                 :            :         /*
    6518                 :            :          * Get the remote host name and port for logging and status display.

    6520                 :         54 :         remote_host[0] = '\0';
    6521                 :         54 :         remote_port[0] = '\0';
    6522 [ -  + ][ -  + ]:         54 :         if (pg_getnameinfo_all(&port->raddr.addr, port->raddr.salen,
    6523                 :         54 :                                                    remote_host, sizeof(remote_host),
    6524                 :         54 :                                                    remote_port, sizeof(remote_port),
    6525                 :         54 :                                            (log_hostname ? 0 : NI_NUMERICHOST) | NI_NUMERICSERV))
    6526                 :            :         {
    6527                 :          0 :                 int                     ret = pg_getnameinfo_all(&port->raddr.addr, port->raddr.salen,
    6528                 :          0 :                                                                                          remote_host, sizeof(remote_host),
    6529                 :          0 :                                                                                          remote_port, sizeof(remote_port),
    6530                 :            :                                                                                          NI_NUMERICHOST | NI_NUMERICSERV);
    6531                 :            : 
    6532         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 if (ret)
    6533         [ #  # ]:          0 :                         ereport(WARNING,
    6534                 :            :                                         (errmsg_internal("pg_getnameinfo_all() failed: %s",
    6535                 :            :                                                                          gai_strerror(ret))));
    6536                 :          0 :         }

    6541                 :            : 
    6542         [ -  + ]:         54 :         if (Log_connections)

    6546                 :            :                                                 remote_port)));

    6553                 :            :

    6578                 :            :          *
    6579                 :            :          * For a walsender, the ps display is set in the following form:
    6580                 :            :          *

    6582                 :            :          *

    6584                 :            :          * as dbname to init_ps_display(). XXX: should add a new variant of

    6595                 :            :          * Done with authentication.  Disable timeout, and prevent SIGTERM/SIGQUIT

    6597                 :            :          */

    6602         [ #  # ]:          0 :         if (Log_connections)
    6603         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(LOG,
    6604                 :            :                                 (errmsg("connection authorized: user=%s database=%s",
    6605                 :            :                                                 port->user_name, port->database_name)));
    6606                 :          0 : }
    6607                 :            : 
    6608                 :            : 
    6609                 :            : /*
    6610                 :            :  * BackendRun -- set up the backend's argument list and invoke PostgresMain()
    6611                 :            :  *
    6612                 :            :  * returns:
    6613                 :            :  *              Shouldn't return at all.
    6614                 :            :  *              If PostgresMain() fails, return status.
    6615                 :            :  */
    6616                 :            : static int
    6617                 :            : BackendRun(Port *port)
    6618                 :            : {
    6619                 :            :         char      **av;
    6620                 :            :         int                     maxac;
    6621                 :            :         int                     ac;
    6622                 :            :         long            secs;
    6623                 :            :         int                     usecs;
    6624                 :            :         int                     i;
    6625                 :            : 
    6626                 :            :         /*
    6627                 :            :          * Don't want backend to be able to see the postmaster random number
    6628                 :            :          * generator state.  We have to clobber the static random_seed *and* start
    6629                 :            :          * a new random sequence in the random() library function.
    6630                 :            :          */
    6631                 :          0 :         random_seed = 0;
    6632                 :          0 :         random_start_time.tv_usec = 0;
    6633                 :            :         /* slightly hacky way to get integer microseconds part of timestamptz */
    6634                 :          0 :         TimestampDifference(0, port->SessionStartTime, &secs, &usecs);
    6635                 :          0 :         srandom((unsigned int) (MyProcPid ^ usecs));
    6636                 :            : 
    6637                 :            :         /*
    6638                 :            :          * Now, build the argv vector that will be given to PostgresMain.
    6639                 :            :          *
    6640                 :            :          * The maximum possible number of commandline arguments that could come
    6641                 :            :          * from ExtraOptions is (strlen(ExtraOptions) + 1) / 2; see
    6642                 :            :          * pg_split_opts().
    6643                 :            :          */
    6644                 :          0 :         maxac = 2;                                      /* for fixed args supplied below */
    6645                 :          0 :         maxac += (strlen(ExtraOptions) + 1) / 2;
    6646                 :            : 
    6647                 :          0 :         av = (char **) MemoryContextAlloc(TopMemoryContext,
    6648                 :            :                                                                           maxac * sizeof(char *));
    6649                 :          0 :         ac = 0;
    6650                 :            : 
    6651                 :          0 :         av[ac++] = "postgres";
    6652                 :            : 
    6653                 :            :         /*
    6654                 :            :          * Pass any backend switches specified with -o on the postmaster's own
    6655                 :            :          * command line.  We assume these are secure.
    6656                 :            :          */
    6657                 :          0 :         pg_split_opts(av, &ac, ExtraOptions);
    6658                 :            : 
    6659                 :          0 :         av[ac] = NULL;
    6660                 :            : 
    6661 [ #  # ][ #  # ]:          0 :         Assert(ac < maxac);
    6662                 :            : 
    6663                 :            :         /*
    6664                 :            :          * Debug: print arguments being passed to backend
    6665                 :            :          */
    6666         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ereport(DEBUG3,
    6667                 :            :                         (errmsg_internal("%s child[%d]: starting with (",
    6668                 :            :                                                          progname, (int) getpid())));
    6669         [ #  # ]:          0 :         for (i = 0; i < ac; ++i)
    6670         [ #  # ]:          0 :                 ereport(DEBUG3,
    6671                 :            :                                 (errmsg_internal("\t%s", av[i])));
    6672         [ #  # ]:          0 :         ereport(DEBUG3,
    6673                 :            :                         (errmsg_internal(")")));
    6674                 :            : 
    6675                 :            :         /*
    6676                 :            :          * Make sure we aren't in PostmasterContext anymore.  (We can't delete it
    6677                 :            :          * just yet, though, because InitPostgres will need the HBA data.)
    6678                 :            :          */
    6679                 :          0 :         MemoryContextSwitchTo(TopMemoryContext);
    6680                 :            : 
    6681                 :          0 :         return PostgresMain(ac, av, port->database_name, port->user_name);
    6682                 :            : }
    6683                 :            : 
    6684                 :            : 
    6685                 :            : #ifdef EXEC_BACKEND
    6686                 :            : 
    6687                 :            : /*
    6688                 :            :  * postmaster_forkexec -- fork and exec a postmaster subprocess
    6689                 :            :  *
    6690                 :            :  * The caller must have set up the argv array already, except for argv[2]
    6691                 :            :  * which will be filled with the name of the temp variable file.
    6692                 :            :  *
    6693                 :            :  * Returns the child process PID, or -1 on fork failure (a suitable error
    6694                 :            :  * message has been logged on failure).
    6695                 :            :  *
    6696                 :            :  * All uses of this routine will dispatch to SubPostmasterMain in the
    6697                 :            :  * child process.
    6698                 :            :  */
    6699                 :            : pid_t
    6700                 :            : postmaster_forkexec(int argc, char *argv[])
    6701                 :            : {
    6702                 :            :         Port            port;
    6703                 :            : 
    6704                 :            :         /* This entry point passes dummy values for the Port variables */
    6705                 :            :         memset(&port, 0, sizeof(port));
    6706                 :            :         return internal_forkexec(argc, argv, &port);
    6707                 :            : }
    6708                 :            : 
    6709                 :            : /*
    6710                 :            :  * backend_forkexec -- fork/exec off a backend process
    6711                 :            :  *
    6712                 :            :  * Some operating systems (WIN32) don't have fork() so we have to simulate
    6713                 :            :  * it by storing parameters that need to be passed to the child and
    6714                 :            :  * then create a new child process.
    6715                 :            :  *
    6716                 :            :  * returns the pid of the fork/exec'd process, or -1 on failure
    6717                 :            :  */
    6718                 :            : static pid_t
    6719                 :            : backend_forkexec(Port *port)
    6720                 :            : {
    6721                 :            :         char       *av[4];
    6722                 :            :         int                     ac = 0;
    6723                 :            : 
    6724                 :            :         av[ac++] = "postgres";
    6725                 :            :         av[ac++] = "--forkbackend";
    6726                 :            :         av[ac++] = NULL;                        /* filled in by internal_forkexec */
    6727                 :            : 
    6728                 :            :         av[ac] = NULL;
    6729                 :            :         Assert(ac < lengthof(av));
    6730                 :            : 
    6731                 :            :         return internal_forkexec(ac, av, port);
    6732                 :            : }
    6733                 :            : 
    6734                 :            : #ifndef WIN32
    6735                 :            : 
    6736                 :            : /*
    6737                 :            :  * internal_forkexec non-win32 implementation
    6738                 :            :  *
    6739                 :            :  * - writes out backend variables to the parameter file
    6740                 :            :  * - fork():s, and then exec():s the child process
    6741                 :            :  */
    6742                 :            : static pid_t
    6743                 :            : internal_forkexec(int argc, char *argv[], Port *port)
    6744                 :            : {
    6745                 :            :         static unsigned long tmpBackendFileNum = 0;
    6746                 :            :         pid_t           pid;
    6747                 :            :         char            tmpfilename[MAXPGPATH];
    6748                 :            :         BackendParameters param;
    6749                 :            :         FILE       *fp;
    6750                 :            : 
    6751                 :            :         if (!save_backend_variables(&param, port))
    6752                 :            :                 return -1;                              /* log made by save_backend_variables */
    6753                 :            : 
    6754                 :            :         /* Calculate name for temp file */
    6755                 :            :         snprintf(tmpfilename, MAXPGPATH, "%s/%s.backend_var.%d.%lu",
    6756                 :            :                          PG_TEMP_FILES_DIR, PG_TEMP_FILE_PREFIX,
    6757                 :            :                          MyProcPid, ++tmpBackendFileNum);
    6758                 :            : 
    6759                 :            :         /* Open file */
    6760                 :            :         fp = AllocateFile(tmpfilename, PG_BINARY_W);
    6761                 :            :         if (!fp)
    6762                 :            :         {
    6763                 :            :                 /* As in OpenTemporaryFile, try to make the temp-file directory */
    6764                 :            :                 mkdir(PG_TEMP_FILES_DIR, S_IRWXU);
    6765                 :            : 
    6766                 :            :                 fp = AllocateFile(tmpfilename, PG_BINARY_W);
    6767                 :            :                 if (!fp)
    6768                 :            :                 {
    6769                 :            :                         ereport(LOG,
    6770                 :            :                                         (errcode_for_file_access(),
    6771                 :            :                                          errmsg("could not create file \"%s\": %m",
    6772                 :            :                                                         tmpfilename)));
    6773                 :            :                         return -1;
    6774                 :            :                 }
    6775                 :            :         }
    6776                 :            : 
    6777                 :            :         if (fwrite(&param, sizeof(param), 1, fp) != 1)
    6778                 :            :         {
    6779                 :            :                 ereport(LOG,
    6780                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    6781                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not write to file \"%s\": %m", tmpfilename)));
    6782                 :            :                 FreeFile(fp);
    6783                 :            :                 return -1;
    6784                 :            :         }
    6785                 :            : 
    6786                 :            :         /* Release file */
    6787                 :            :         if (FreeFile(fp))
    6788                 :            :         {
    6789                 :            :                 ereport(LOG,
    6790                 :            :                                 (errcode_for_file_access(),
    6791                 :            :                                  errmsg("could not write to file \"%s\": %m", tmpfilename)));
    6792                 :            :                 return -1;
    6793                 :            :         }
    6794                 :            : 
    6795                 :            :         /* Make sure caller set up argv properly */
    6796                 :            :         Assert(argc >= 3);
    6797                 :            :         Assert(argv[argc] == NULL);
    6798                 :            :         Assert(strncmp(argv[1], "--fork", 6) == 0);
    6799                 :            :         Assert(argv[2] == NULL);
    6800                 :            : 
    6801                 :            :         /* Insert temp file name after --fork argument */
    6802                 :            :         argv[2] = tmpfilename;
    6803                 :            : 
    6804                 :            :         /* Fire off execv in child */
    6805                 :            :         if ((pid = fork_process()) == 0)
    6806                 :            :         {
    6807                 :            :                 if (execv(postgres_exec_path, argv) < 0)
    6808                 :            :                 {
    6809                 :            :                         ereport(LOG,
    6810                 :            :                                         (errmsg("could not execute server process \"%s\": %m",
    6811                 :            :                                                         postgres_exec_path)));
    6812                 :            :                         /* We're already in the child process here, can't return */
    6813                 :            :                         exit(1);
    6814                 :            :                 }
    6815                 :            :         }
    6816                 :            : 
    6817                 :            :         return pid;                                     /* Parent returns pid, or -1 on fork failure */
    6818                 :            : }
    6819                 :            : #else                                                   /* WIN32 */
    6820                 :            : 
    6821                 :            : /*
    6822                 :            :  * internal_forkexec win32 implementation
    6823                 :            :  *
    6824                 :            :  * - starts backend using CreateProcess(), in suspended state
    6825                 :            :  * - writes out backend variables to the parameter file
    6826                 :            :  *      - during this, duplicates handles and sockets required for
    6827                 :            :  *        inheritance into the new process
    6828                 :            :  * - resumes execution of the new process once the backend parameter
    6829                 :            :  *       file is complete.
    6830                 :            :  */
    6831                 :            : static pid_t
    6832                 :            : internal_forkexec(int argc, char *argv[], Port *port)
    6833                 :            : {
    6834                 :            :         STARTUPINFO si;
    6835                 :            :         PROCESS_INFORMATION pi;
    6836                 :            :         int                     i;
    6837                 :            :         int                     j;
    6838                 :            :         char            cmdLine[MAXPGPATH * 2];
